text:
Good evening, friends. It's certainly a privilege to be here tonight in this meeting again. Coming in the love of the Lord Jesus to minister to His dear people that's so much in need, and may His blessings rest upon each and every one of you.
A bad night on the outside. Or, it seems that way maybe, to me being a Southerner, but up here it may be all right. I was certainly happy tonight when some of the brethren here at the church come and got me; they've been so nice to me. I appreciate all your kindness.
text_no:
God kveld, venner. Det er virkelig et privilegium å være her igjen i dette møtet i kveld. Jeg kommer i kjærligheten fra Herren Jesus for å tjene Hans kjære folk som har så stort behov, og må Hans velsignelser hvile over hver og en av dere.
Det er en dårlig natt ute. Eller, det kan virke slik for meg som er fra Sør, men her oppe kan det være helt i orden. Jeg var virkelig glad i kveld da noen av brødrene her i menigheten kom og hentet meg; de har vært så hyggelige mot meg. Jeg setter pris på all vennlighet dere har vist.

text:
I've got a little Chevrolet truck I've been coming back and forth in, and since I been here they'd come and get me in a big Oldsmobile. So I certainly feel like I've been honored to be in your meetings, to be here.
We've got, I think, this being our last night at the church, and tomorrow night we move over for our closing night at the hall, at the Andy Cotter, I believe, Johnson City Auditorium, or the brothers announced it. I don't know much about the place, and I couldn't find it, I guess, but we look it up tomorrow to know how to get to it.
Now, tonight we are gathered for one sole purpose, and that's to magnify Jesus Christ to the people. I don't know no other alternative that I would be here tonight.
text_no:
Jeg har en liten Chevrolet-lastebil som jeg har kommet og gått med, men siden jeg kom hit, har de hentet meg i en stor Oldsmobile. Jeg føler meg virkelig beæret over å få delta i møtene deres og være her.
Jeg tror dette er vår siste kveld i menigheten, og i morgen kveld flytter vi over til hallen for vår avslutningskveld. Det skjer på Andy Cotter, jeg tror det er Johnson City Auditorium, som brødrene nevnte. Jeg vet ikke mye om stedet, og jeg kunne nok ikke funnet det selv, men vi skal finne det ut i morgen slik at vi vet hvor vi skal.
I kveld er vi samlet for ett eneste formål, og det er å opphøye Jesus Kristus for folk. Jeg kan ikke tenke meg noe annet grunnen til at jeg skulle være her i kveld.

text:
I'm a married man, have a family, and I really love my home, what little I get to be in my home. They've moved me up to the new parsonage, at the church's parsonage seven years ago, when they built it. And I have never eaten one time in the parsonage without the shades, the Venetian blinds turned down in the kitchen. The people standing on the porch, in the yard, and around like that. I've got two children that's almost neurotics from just all hours of the night up going, when I'm at home. People coming, see there…
If it was just like from here at Binghamton, it wouldn't make much difference; but it's the whole world, you see. They gather in there. I just heard from my wife, and they're sitting in those hotels and motels there from all over the country everywhere. Wanted to know just what day, what time Friday morning, I'll arrive, you see.
And you can imagine what it'll be. And then through the holidays people coming out. And that… We put in the papers not to do it, but people are sick, and they… Could you imagine you could feel at ease, and a little baby laying out in the yard, maybe in a car crying? Some old poor sick mother there that maybe a little word of prayer would change the whole situation. The only way to do is just … the brethren get me and just keep me away from home. That's the only thing. I have to get off somewhere to kind of get if off my mind. It's not good for you if you keep that up.
Jesus told His apostles, "We got to come apart into the desert." And when you're out there and you got to do something just to keep it off your mind, you'd be thinking about it anyhow. And the prayer of the people has such an effect. Do you believe that? Certainly. Oh!
text_no:
Jeg er en gift mann, har en familie, og jeg elsker virkelig hjemmet mitt, selv om jeg sjelden får være der. For sju år siden flyttet de meg til den nye presteboligen, som ble bygget ved menighetens område. Jeg har aldri spist én eneste gang i presteboligen uten å trekke ned persiennene i kjøkkenet. Folk står på verandaen, i hagen og rundt omkring. Jeg har to barn som nesten er nevrotiske av alle forstyrrelsene, spesielt om natten når jeg er hjemme. Folk kommer til enhver tid...
Det ville ikke gjort stor forskjell om det bare var folk fra Binghamton, men dette skjer hele verden over. De samles der. Jeg hørte nettopp fra min kone om folk som venter på hoteller og moteller fra hele landet, og de ønsker å vite hvilken dag og tid jeg ankommer på fredag morgen.
Du kan forestille deg hvordan det blir gjennom høytidene når folk kommer. Vi har satt inn i avisene at de ikke skal gjøre det, men folk er syke... Kan du tenke deg å føle deg rolig når du hører et lite barn ligge ute i hagen eller i en bil og gråte? En syk mor som kanskje et lite bønneord kunne ha forandret hele situasjonen for? Den eneste løsningen er at brødrene holder meg unna hjemmet. Det er det eneste som fungerer. Jeg må dra et sted for å få sinnet fri. Det er ikke bra å fortsette slik.
Jesus fortalte sine apostler: "Vi må trekke oss tilbake til ørkenen." Og når du er der ute, må du gjøre noe for å få tankene bort fra det, selv om du uansett tenker på det. Bønnen fra folket har en slik effekt. Tror du ikke det? Absolutt. Å, ja!

text:
When I return, the Lord willing, I'll be able to tell you experiences of things of that type, that I have had experience with. For instance, while it's just on my mind, before I turn to my lesson, tonight, being that we just got a couple nights together. We're not in no big hurry, are we?
I was here just recently, old Brother Bosworth… How many knows F. F. Bosworth? My, nearly everybody knows him. And since Mr. Baxter has resigned, he went into some business that he couldn't take care of these campaigns, why Mr. Bosworth is the floor manager in the meetings now, in the big meetings. A very dear brother, oh, sweet humble. You just love him. You just can't keep from loving Brother Bosworth. Such a saintly, godly, old brother. And he was in Africa, and he was supposed to be on the East Coast, and then we was to go to the West Coast together; that's after we had been there once, where we had the great meetings.
text_no:
Når jeg kommer tilbake, hvis Herren vil, vil jeg kunne fortelle dere om erfaringer jeg har hatt av lignende slag. For eksempel, mens jeg tenker på det nå, før jeg går over til min leksjon i kveld. Siden vi bare har et par netter sammen, er vi vel ikke i noen stor hast, er vi?
For ikke så lenge siden, var gamle Bror Bosworth her... Hvor mange kjenner til F. F. Bosworth? Å, nesten alle kjenner ham. Etter at Mr. Baxter trakk seg tilbake, gikk han inn i en virksomhet som gjorde at han ikke kunne ta seg av disse kampanjene. Derfor er nå Bror Bosworth gulvansvarlig i de store møtene. En svært kjær bror, åh, så søt og ydmyk. Du bare elsker ham. Du kan ikke unngå å elske Bror Bosworth. For en hellig, gudfryktig, gammel bror. Han var i Afrika, og skulle være på Østkysten, og deretter skulle vi reise til Vestkysten sammen. Dette var etter at vi hadde vært der en gang tidligere, der vi holdt de store møtene.

text:
And one night, the room had been so crowded all day long, I was just so weary I could… I told my wife, I said, "If anybody else calls," it was on Saturday night, I said, "tell them just go to the church in the morning, and I will pray for them down there, because I'm just getting so tired, honey," I said, "I just can't hold myself together." And getting so nervous, I was just getting upset.
Well, she's thirty-five and completely gray headed. You can imagine: she stands between me and the public there. So you can imagine what it is, and just went gray in the last year or two. But… And she got the people away. When they cleaned up the room, after everyone was gone, we jumped in the car, real quick, and taken a little ride. We went down below New Albany's knobs, not mountains, just kind of little mountains, what we call "knobs" there. And it's kind of rough along this side, and I was driving along there. All at once my windshield just become completely white. And next thing I knew, she was saying, "Billy, what's the matter with you?"
And we had drove about a mile around those cliffs. And I knew nothing about it. It had been a vision. And I stopped the car real quick. We was way up in the wooded country. I said, "Sweetheart, I got to get out here at the road and pray for Brother Bosworth, just at once." I said, "I see him get off of a train at Durban, South Africa, and he was stricken. I seen them pick him up and put on stretchers, and he's in the hospital and very seriously. And I must pray for him at once."
text_no:
En natt var rommet fylt hele dagen, og jeg var så utmattet at jeg sa til min kone, "Hvis det ringer noen flere," det var lørdagskveld, "si at de må gå til menigheten i morgen, og jeg vil be for dem der nede, for jeg er så sliten, kjære," sa jeg, "jeg klarer ikke mer." Jeg var så nervøs og opprørt.
Hun er trettifem og helt gråhåret. Du kan forestille deg: hun står mellom meg og publikum. Så du kan tenke deg hvordan det er, hun ble gråhåret bare i løpet av det siste året eller to. Hun fikk sendt bort folkene. Da rommet var ryddet etter at alle hadde gått, hoppet vi raskt i bilen og tok en liten kjøretur. Vi kjørte ned til det vi kaller "knobs" ved New Albany, som er små fjell eller høydedrag. Det er ganske ulendt terreng langs denne siden, og jeg kjørte der.
Plutselig ble vinduet helt hvitt. Neste jeg husker, var at hun sa, "Billy, hva er galt med deg?" Vi hadde kjørt omtrent en kilometer langs klippene, og jeg ante ikke hva som hadde skjedd. Det hadde vært en visjon. Jeg stoppet bilen raskt. Vi var langt inne i skogen. Jeg sa, "Kjære, jeg må ut her ved veien og be for Bror Bosworth straks." Jeg sa, "Jeg ser ham gå av et tog i Durban, Sør-Afrika, og han ble rammet. Jeg ser dem løfte ham på en båre og bringe ham til sykehuset, og han er alvorlig syk. Jeg må be for ham med en gang."

text:
Well, we stopped the car; I got outside, went over to the edge of the woods and knelt down, prayed to God to help brother. And then that was Saturday night, and the following Sunday night, I hadn't left the house yet, and some friends was in the house.
And we have four phones to answer on, you can just about imagine what it is, the calls. And that phone only takes long distance. And they said the Louisville operator of the Western Union was calling. And she said… I went to the phone, and she said, "Reverend Branham, I have a telegram from Durban, South Africa, from Mister, Reverend Doctor Lydare." And said, "Pray for Brother Bosworth at once. Was stricken after stepping off a train yesterday. In the hospital expecting him to die at any moment." Like that.
text_no:
Vi stoppet bilen; jeg gikk ut, bort til skogkanten, knelte ned og ba til Gud om å hjelpe bror. Dette var lørdag kveld. Den påfølgende søndagskvelden hadde jeg ennå ikke forlatt huset, og noen venner var på besøk.
Vi har fire telefoner som vi må besvare, og dere kan bare forestille dere alle samtalene. En av telefonene tar kun imot fjernanrop. De sa at Louisville-operatøren fra Western Union ringte. Jeg gikk til telefonen, og hun sa: "Pastor Branham, jeg har et telegram fra Durban, Sør-Afrika, fra Herr, Pastor Doctor Lydare." Hun sa: "Be for Bror Bosworth umiddelbart. Han ble rammet etter å ha gått av et tog i går. Han er på sykehuset og forventes å dø hvert øyeblikk."

text:
I said, "Operator, could you trace that back and tell me when that left Durban?"
She said, "Yes, sir." She called me in a few moments, and told me what time it left Durban. I've got off of my travels so many times, the Pan American gives a little chart, and it gives the hours, and so forth. And what had happened, that Angel of God had beat that telegram there, twenty-four hours, just exactly. And the time I got a call through the next day, Brother Bosworth done been healed, was up and going, you see. So, he was healed before the telegram could get there. The sovereignty of God: the Angel of the Lord beat the telegram here, twenty-four hours, see.
text_no:
Jeg sa: "Operatør, kan du spore det tilbake og fortelle meg når det ble sendt fra Durban?"
Hun svarte: "Ja, herr." Hun ringte meg etter noen minutter og fortalte meg tidspunktet det ble sendt fra Durban. Jeg har gått av flyreisen så mange ganger; Pan American gir oss et lite kart med klokkeslett og så videre. Det som hadde skjedd, var at Guds Engel hadde slått telegrammet med tjuefire timer, helt eksakt. Da jeg fikk tatt en telefon dagen etter, var Bror Bosworth allerede blitt helbredet og var oppe og gikk. Han ble altså helbredet før telegrammet kom frem. Guds suverenitet: Herrens Engel slo telegrammet med tjuefire timer.

text:
Oh, it's past knowing. He's just… We just love Him, that's all. I just … with all my heart.
Now, as a man, my words are so limited, like any other man, but His Word is so real and cannot fail, I always like to read some of His words before I say anything about the night service.
text_no:
Åh, det er utenfor vår fatteevne. Vi bare elsker Ham, det er alt. Jeg bare ... med hele mitt hjerte.
Som menneske er mine ord begrensede, slik som enhver andres, men Hans Ord er så virkelig og kan ikke feile. Jeg liker alltid å lese noe fra Hans Ord før jeg sier noe om kveldens møte.

text:
Now, in the second chapter of Saint Luke, and beginning with the 25th verse, we read these verses out of God's Word. And then we'll ask Him to add His blessing to it, and then we'll talk a little while, and then our prayer line.
Now, second chapter, 25th verse, I'll start reading.
And, behold, there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name was Simeon; and the same man was just and devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel: and the Holy Ghost was upon him.
And it was revealed unto him by the Holy Ghost, that he should not see death, before he had seen the Lord's Christ.
And he came by the Spirit into the temple: and then the child was … when they brought … the child Jesus, to do for him after the custom of the law,
Then took he him up in his arms, and blessed God and said,
Lord, now lettest … thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word:
For my eyes have seen thy salvation,
A wonderful text, for this time, yuletides of the year, of Christmas, the Lord Jesus Christ's birth. Shall we speak to Him just a moment, while we bow our heads.
text_no:
I det andre kapittelet av Evangeliet etter Lukas, fra vers 25, leser vi disse versene fra Guds Ord. Deretter ber vi Ham velsigne teksten, før vi snakker litt, etterfulgt av bønnekøen.
Nå begynner vi å lese fra andre kapittel, vers 25:
*Og se, det var en mann i Jerusalem som het Simeon, og denne mannen var rettferdig og gudfryktig og ventet på Israels trøst, og Den Hellige Ånd var over ham.*
*Og det var blitt åpenbart for ham av Den Hellige Ånd at han ikke skulle se døden før han hadde sett Herrens Salvede.*
*Han kom ved Ånden til templet, og da foreldrene brakte inn barnet Jesus for å gjøre med Ham etter lovens skikk, tok han Ham i sin favn og priste Gud og sa,*
*Herre, nå lar Du Din tjener fare i fred, etter Ditt Ord,*
*for mine øyne har sett Din frelse.*
En vidunderlig tekst for denne tiden, i juletiden hvor vi minnes Herrens Jesu Kristi fødsel. La oss tale med Ham et øyeblikk mens vi bøyer våre hoder.

text:
Our loving Father, we thank Thee tonight for Thy love, that sent the Son of God from glory to earth, to become sin. And tonight with our throne blessings in our heart, by His grace, to know that we were sinners, and He came and took our place, and died as a sinner. Died in my place, He died my death at Calvary. He died all of our deaths at Calvary. And the sin … me as a sinner, He represented me in His death at Calvary. He represented every believer tonight in His death at Calvary.
And God, reconciling us, and His love to us, raised Him up again for our justification. And therefore, being raised up with Him, we're seated tonight in heavenly places in Christ Jesus with every blessing that God promised, is our redemptive blessings in Him. We thank Thee for this. And You, by faith, Father, foreseeing it, knowing what would take place, has blessed us together, with Christ. Every blessing that He died for is ours right now, for we're seated in Christ. We're resurrected with Christ. And now, we're seated in Him, as believers, in this heavenly place in Christ Jesus.
text_no:
Vår kjærlige Far, vi takker Deg i kveld for Din kjærlighet som sendte Guds Sønn fra herligheten til jorden for å bli synd. I kveld, med våre tronevelsignelser i hjertet, ved Hans nåde, vet vi at vi var syndere, og Han kom og tok vår plass og døde som en synder. Han døde i mitt sted, Han døde min død på Golgata. Han døde våre død på Golgata. Og synden.. meg som en synder, Han representerte meg i Sin død på Golgata. Han representerte hver troende i kveld i Sin død på Golgata.
Gud, forsonet med oss gjennom Hans kjærlighet, oppreiste Ham igjen til vår rettferdiggjørelse. Og derfor, oppreist med Ham, er vi i kveld satt i himmelske steder i Kristus Jesus, med alle velsignelser som Gud lovte, våre forløsende velsignelser i Ham. Vi takker Deg for dette. Og Du, ved tro, Far, forutseende det, visste hva som ville skje, har velsignet oss sammen med Kristus. Hver velsignelse Han døde for er vår akkurat nå, for vi er satt i Kristus. Vi er oppreist med Kristus. Og nå, som troende, er vi satt i Ham, i dette himmelske stedet i Kristus Jesus.

text:
O Father, it shakes our hearts to know, the sense mind could not understand it. It's beyond any of our senses to comprehend what this means; that we, tonight, are now seated in Christ. Every blessing that He died for is ours right now; we have it. O God, help us to have faith tonight to use Your faith, that You said it was ours. And let us claim it tonight, Lord, every God-given privilege that we have. For You have bought us, called us by grace, washed us in the blood of Thy Son, the Lord Jesus, and has cleansed us up, filled us with the Holy Spirit, set us in Christ Jesus, here tonight to claim anything, any redemptive blessing.
And our enemy tonight is just a bluff. For Christ robbed him of everything he was, at Calvary, when He stripped of everything---power---that he ever had. For God was … man was reconciled to God at that time through the blood of Jesus Christ when He fully paid the price and the full supreme offering was made; and God was pleased with it. And tonight, we're resurrected in Him.
Now, we thank Thee for this. Knowing this, that no matter what comes or goes, we're in Him. Those who He has justified, He has already glorified. And the Father in His own faith, believing that we will hold to our redemptive blessings, and the righteousness of the Lord Jesus Christ, foresees us yonder in eternity, glorified together with Him, with glorified bodies, free from sickness, free from death. And to live with Him as His dear beloved children, through all ceaseless ages and eons of time to come.
Bless us, Lord, tonight. May our hearts be moved as the Holy Spirit comes down the aisles of our being, and cleanses us from all doubt and unrighteousness. For we ask it in His name. Amen.
text_no:
O Far, det rører våre hjerter å vite at vårt sinn ikke kan forstå dette. Det er hinsides noen av våre sanser å fatte hva dette betyr; at vi, i kveld, nå er plassert i Kristus. Hver velsignelse Han døde for er vår akkurat nå; vi har den. O Gud, hjelp oss å ha tro i kveld til å bruke Din tro, som Du sa var vår. La oss kreve det i kveld, Herre, hvert Gud-gitt privilegium vi har. For Du har kjøpt oss, kalt oss ved nåde, vasket oss i blodet til Din Sønn, Herren Jesus, og renset oss, fylt oss med Den Hellige Ånd, satt oss i Kristus Jesus, her i kveld for å kreve enhver forløsende velsignelse.
Og vår fiende i kveld er bare en bløff. For Kristus frarøvet ham alt han var på Golgata, da Han ribbet ham for alt — all makt — han noensinne hadde. For Gud og mennesket ble forsonet på den tiden gjennom Jesu Kristi blod da Han fullt ut betalte prisen, og det fullkomne, høyeste offeret ble gitt; og Gud var fornøyd med det. Og i kveld er vi oppreist i Ham.
Nå takker vi Deg for dette. Vi vet at uansett hva som kommer eller går, så er vi i Ham. De som Han har rettferdiggjort, har Han allerede herliggjort. Og Faderen i Sin egen tro, troende at vi skal holde fast ved våre forløsende velsignelser og Herren Jesu Kristi rettferdighet, forutser oss der borte i evigheten, herliggjort sammen med Ham, med herliggjorte kropper, fri fra sykdom og død. Og leve med Ham som Hans kjære elskede barn, gjennom alle tider og evigheter som kommer.
Velsign oss, Herre, i kveld. Må våre hjerter røres når Den Hellige Ånd kommer ned gangene i vår væren og renser oss fra all tvil og urettferdighet. For vi ber om det i Hans navn. Amen.

text:
Now, someone has been asking why I do not pray for more than I do at night. I might explain that just a moment. See, friends, you don't realize what that does to me. How many knows that that vision takes more out of me than anything else that I know of. The prophet, Daniel, saw one vision and was troubled at his head for many days. Is that right? Look, did you all know that most all poets and prophets and inspired people were considered neurotics? You know: crazy? That's right.
Look. For the people here that probably are not Christians, how about the great folk-song writer, gave America some of its most lovely folk songs, was Stephen Foster. He wrote: The Old Folks at Home, Old Kentucky Home, Suwannee River (many of those), Old Black Joe---many of those great inspiring songs.
text_no:
Noen har spurt hvorfor jeg ikke ber mer om nettene. La meg forklare det kort. Venner, dere forstår nok ikke hvilken innvirkning det har på meg. Hvor mange vet at visjoner tar mer på meg enn noe annet jeg kjenner til? Profeten Daniel så én visjon og var plaget i mange dager. Er det riktig? Vet dere at de fleste diktere, profeter og inspirerte personer ofte ble ansett som nevrotikere? Dere vet: gale? Det stemmer.
For de som kanskje ikke er kristne her, kan vi se på den store folkesangskriveren som ga Amerika noen av sine vakreste folkesanger, Stephen Foster. Han skrev: “The Old Folks at Home”, “Old Kentucky Home”, “Suwannee River”, og “Old Black Joe”, blant mange andre inspirerende sanger.

text:
Here not long ago… Old Kentucky Home is just across the river from me. I was over there sitting at the little bench that he was supposed … or, table; it was valued at a hundred thousand dollars many years ago, when the World's Fair was held at St. Louis. And he was … here it came, that song. There was his picture, and with the seraphim, supposed to touch him, give him inspiration. I thought, Yes, Mr. Foster, you had it here in the head, not in the heart.
But, if you notice every time that he would write a song, that inspiration would leave him, he'd get on a drunk. And finally, now, just don't condemn the man too quick, see, till you understand. Not being what he should, he'd get on a drunk. He'd leave that inspiration. And finally, when he come out from under the inspiration one day, called his servant and took a razor and committed suicide. He didn't know where he was at.
"Well," you say, "yes…"
How many of you knows William Cowper? Many of you knows his famous song: "There is a fountain filled with blood drawn from Immanuel's veins; when sinners plunged beneath the flood, lose all their guilty stains."
text_no:
For ikke lenge siden satt jeg ved et lite bord i Old Kentucky Home, like over elven fra der jeg bor. Bordet ble verdsatt til hundre tusen dollar for mange år siden, da Verdensutstillingen ble holdt i St. Louis. Mens jeg satt der, kom den sangen til meg. Der var hans bilde, og med serafene som angivelig skulle berøre ham og gi ham inspirasjon. Jeg tenkte: Ja, Mr. Foster, du hadde det i hodet, men ikke i hjertet.
Men hvis du legger merke til det, hver gang han skrev en sang, forlot inspirasjonen ham og han endte opp med å drikke. Ikke døm mannen for raskt før du forstår. Uten å være det han burde ha vært, endte han opp med å drikke. Han forlot inspirasjonen, og en dag, da han kom ut av inspirasjonen, ropte han på tjeneren sin, tok en barberhøvel og begikk selvmord. Han visste ikke hvor han var.
"Vel," kan du si, "ja…"
Hvor mange av dere kjenner til William Cowper? Mange av dere kjenner hans kjente sang: "There is a fountain filled with blood drawn from Immanuel's veins; when sinners plunged beneath the flood, lose all their guilty stains."

text:
I stood by his grave in London, England, not long ago, and I looked down there, and I thought, Yes, Mr. Cowper. I just read his history. You know, after he had inspiration, and wrote that song… When he was up there it was all right, but when he begin to come out of it, he tried to find the river to commit suicide; it was too foggy; the driver couldn't get him to it to commit suicide after writing that famous song: There is a fountain filled with blood drawn from Immanuel's veins; when sinners plunged beneath the flood.
You say, "That's poets." How about prophets? When Jonah received inspiration and went into a city with a million people in it, and was so wicked that they were very wicked. And he prophesied with such power, after God gave him the message to give the people, when he was anointed prophet, to go down there and foretell, "Within forty days would God destroy the city." And it went through with such power until they put sackcloth on their animals; repented in such a way.
And that man went up on the hill and asked God to take his own life, and they put little worm to cut the gourd tree down. Is that right? That was prophet.
And look at Elijah, who called fire out of the heavens and rain out of the heaven at the same day, when it hadn't rained for three years and six months; killed four hundred priests himself that very afternoon. And as soon as the inspiration left him, went running to the wilderness, and wandered out there, not knowing where he was at for forty days and nights, and God found him pulled back in a cave somewhere. And a small voice said … asked him what he was doing back in that cave. Is that right? That's prophets, see. Inspired!
text_no:
Jeg stod ved graven hans i London, England, for ikke lenge siden. Jeg så ned og tenkte: Ja, Mr. Cowper. Jeg hadde nettopp lest historien hans. Etter å ha fått inspirasjon til å skrive den sangen... Da han var oppløftet, var alt i orden. Men da han begynte å falle ned igjen, forsøkte han å finne elven for å ta sitt eget liv. Det var for tåkete, og kusken kunne ikke finne veien dit etter at han hadde skrevet den berømte sangen: «There is a fountain filled with blood drawn from Immanuel's veins; when sinners plunged beneath the flood.»
Du sier: "Det er poeter." Hva med profeter? Da Jona mottok inspirasjon og gikk inn i en by med en million mennesker, en meget ond by. Han profeterte med slik kraft etter at Gud hadde gitt ham budskapet. Da han var salvet som profet, forutsa han med stor makt: "Om førti dager vil Gud ødelegge byen." Det slo slik gjennom at de kledde dyrene sine i sekkeklede og omvendte seg i en slik grad.
Da gikk Jona opp på et fjell og ba Gud om å ta hans liv. De satte en liten orm til å ødelegge kikajontreet. Er det riktig? Det var en profet.
Og se på Elia, som kalte ild ned fra himmelen og regn samme dag, etter at det ikke hadde regnet på tre år og seks måneder. Han drepte fire hundre prester den ettermiddagen. Så snart inspirasjonen forlot ham, løp han til ørkenen og vandret der i førti dager og netter, uten å vite hvor han var, før Gud fant ham dypt inne i en hule. En stille stemme spurte ham hva han gjorde der inne. Er det riktig? Det er profeter. Inspirert!

text: Oh, Christians, most all Christianity in the way we see it today is so shallow; it's like bubble dancing. You speak of Spirit and know very little of it. That's right. You don't get down and deal with the actual facts of it, you see. That's the reason the theologians today just know it from a Word standpoint, and they approach it like that, with a sense knowledge, and know nothing about the indwelling Spirit, the power, see. They don't deal with Spirit. They just look at it and say, "Well, I believe it's this." You draw your own conceptions. That's the reason they make such fatal mistakes.
text_no: Å, kristne, nesten all kristendom slik vi ser den i dag, er så overfladisk; det er som å danse på bobler. Dere snakker om Ånden, men vet veldig lite om Den. Det stemmer. Dere går ikke i dybden og håndterer de faktiske realitetene. Det er grunnen til at dagens teologer bare kjenner Det fra et tekstlig perspektiv og nærmer seg Det med en intellektuell forståelse, uten å vite noe om Den indre Ånden og kraften. De tar ikke tak i Ånden. De ser bare på Den og sier: "Vel, jeg tror Det er slik." Dere danner deres egne forestillinger. Det er grunnen til at de gjør så fatale feil.

text:
Sometimes in the meetings … I say this: Someone said to me, "Brother Branham, why don't you…?" I got a little letter here not long ago, and it said good criticism. Said, "Brother Branham," said, "your meeting is head and shoulders above anything I've ever seen. But," said, "the thing of it is, that you're just so lazy, and won't pray for God's sick children. He had to raise up Oral Roberts to pray for His children." Said, "Oral will pray for about five hundred while you're praying for one."
I said, "That's true. But Brother Roberts is God's servant; and he's doing just what God told him to do, and I'm doing the same thing: just what God told me to do." That's right. Sure. Brother Roberts, if he's a true man of God, which I believe Brother Roberts is, a real man of God, and then Brother Allen, Brother… All these brothers are real Christian brothers. I believe every one of them. I believe every man that calls on Jesus Christ is my brother. And I'm right with them a hundred percent to back them up in anything. As long as it's in the Bible, and right, I'm right behind them. If they get out of the Bible, I still won't condemn them. I love them just the same. And go to them and try to see if we can't get the thing straightened up now.
text_no:
Under møtene sier jeg noen ganger dette: Noen sa til meg: "Bror Branham, hvorfor gjør du ikke...?" Jeg fikk et brev her for en stund siden, som inneholdt god kritikk. Det sto: "Bror Branham, møtene dine er mye bedre enn noe annet jeg har sett. Men," sto det, "du er så lat og vil ikke be for Guds syke barn. Han måtte reise opp Oral Roberts for å be for Sine barn." Det sto, "Oral vil be for omtrent fem hundre mens du ber for én."
Jeg sa, "Det er sant. Men Bror Roberts er Guds tjener og gjør akkurat det Gud har sagt han skal gjøre, og jeg gjør det samme: akkurat det Gud har befalt meg å gjøre." Det er riktig, absolutt. Bror Roberts, hvis han er en sann Guds mann, noe jeg tror han er, er en ekte Guds mann. Også Bror Allen og alle de andre brødrene er ekte kristne brødre. Jeg tror på hver eneste en av dem. Jeg tror enhver som påkaller Jesu Kristi navn er min bror. Jeg støtter dem fullt ut i alt de gjør, så lenge det er i samsvar med Bibelen. Hvis de avviker fra Bibelen, vil jeg fortsatt ikke fordømme dem. Jeg elsker dem like mye, og vil gå til dem for å prøve å få saken ordnet opp.

text:
Now, on these things… But many times in the meetings, I get what goes through the line. Brother Roberts and them, they just real strong. Their meetings are faith. It's just with real predominating faith. There's a little brother here, a business man, in this city, by the name of George Gardner. You talk about a faith, he's got it. But what he does, he's just a babe in Christ, and he's not had many up's and down's, and knocks. So he just reaches out there by faith and grab it and it's on God's Word, he says, "The Word said so, and that settles it." That's the way to do it, see.
Now, those things… That's the way Brother Roberts does. That's the way Brother Allen does. That's the way Brother Coe does. That's the way we all do, see. It's faith, faith, faith.
Well, now, sometimes the people in the meeting, they get a hold of some things that they can't… There's something… But if you've done something in your life, or never done something that you should have done, you could pray over you all you wanted to, and anoint you till you died, and it'll never leave you, see. That's true.
text_no:
Ofte i møtene får jeg innblikk i hva som skjer i bønnekøen. Bror Roberts og de andre er svært sterke. Deres møter preges av overveldende tro. Det er en liten bror her, en forretningsmann i denne byen, ved navn George Gardner. Når det gjelder tro, så har han det virkelig. Men han er bare en nykommer i Kristus og har ikke opplevd mange opp- og nedturer enda. Så han griper bare ut i tro og holder fast ved Guds Ord. Han sier: "Ordet sa det, og det avgjør saken." Det er slik det skal gjøres.
Dette er også metoden Bror Roberts bruker. Det er hvordan Bror Allen gjør det, Bror Coe gjør det, og hvordan vi alle gjør det. Det handler om tro, tro, tro.
Noen ganger tar folk på møtene til seg visse ting de ikke kan håndtere. Men om du har gjort noe i livet ditt, eller unnlatt å gjøre noe du burde ha gjort, kan du bli bedt over så mye du vil og bli salvet til du dør, men det vil aldri forlate deg. Det er sant.

text: See, before… In the meeting it's slow. But you have to find the cause before you can find the cure. If you go to a doctor and tell him you're having persistent headaches, and he gives you an aspirin, sends you back home, he's not a doctor. He's trying to get rid of you; trying to get you out of the office. If he's a real doctor, he will take you and examine your blood, and your pressure and everything else, and hunt till he finds the cause, and then start working from there. That's a real doctor, see. But if he just bounces you off like that, that's not a doctor, see. He's not a good doctor anyhow, 'cause he should be … his patients should be like his own family, that he'd try to hunt it down, see where it's at. A man's got the thing on his heart. Well, now, that's the same thing here, see.
text_no: Se, før... På møtene går det sakte. Du må finne årsaken før du kan finne kuren. Hvis du går til en lege med vedvarende hodepine, og han gir deg en aspirin og sender deg hjem, er han ikke en riktig lege. Han prøver bare å bli kvitt deg; få deg ut av kontoret. En ekte lege vil ta deg inn, undersøke blodet ditt, blodtrykket og alt annet, og lete til Han finner årsaken, for så å begynne behandlingen derfra. Det er en ekte lege. Men om han bare avviser deg på den måten, er han ikke en god lege. Pasientene hans bør være som hans egen familie, som han ville forsøke å finne løsningen for. Dette er det samme her.

text:
Now, here not long ago, just give you a little case. (Am I taking up too much of the time on these testimonies?) A little case, there was a little woman…
A Methodist preacher down in New Albany, Indiana, which is a very dear friend of mine… I had the Baptist tabernacle in Jeffersonville, and we were brothers, if there ever was. And he'd always… If I got a hold of somebody that didn't believe in immersing and wanted to be sprinkled, I'd send him down. I said, "You go down to his church, Brother Len, because you'd make a better member down there, because I'd say he's a real man. And he's a man of God. But he believes in sprinkling, and I'm a Baptist." And so, I'd take him down. If he found somebody, I'd say, "Now, it's awful dry down there, you see."
So, if he'd find somebody that wanted to be immersed instead of being sprinkled, he'd say, "Go up and see Brother Billy." Said, "Now, he'll drown you. He's a Baptist."---but we just had friendship like that with each other.
Well, he was a good man. And he said, after I come in here about … it's been about two years ago, a little over, he said, "Billy, all the way around the world you go, and then leave your poor brother sit down here." Said, "Why don't you come down and hold a meeting for me?"
And I said, "Well, brother, when I come home, I'm trying to rest my mind, and things from the prayer of the sick."
He said, "Well, Brother Branham," he said, "I won't ask you to pray for one person, if you just come down and preach for us one night."
Well, he had a radio program, and he announced it on the radio that morning, and that night, church about like this, and they were everywhere, and out on the streets.
text_no:
La meg gi dere et lite eksempel fra ikke så lenge siden. (Tar jeg for mye tid med disse vitnesbyrdene?) En liten hendelse: Det var en kvinne...
En metodistforkynner i New Albany, Indiana, som er en kjær venn av meg... Jeg hadde baptistmenigheten i Jeffersonville, og vi var brødre, hvis det noen gang har vært noen. Og alltid... Hvis jeg kom over noen som ikke trodde på neddykking og heller ønsket å bli sprinklet, sendte jeg dem nedover. Jeg sa, "Gå til hans menighet, Bror Len, fordi du ville passe bedre der nede, ettersom han er en virkelig mann. Og han er en Guds mann. Men han tror på sprinkling, og jeg er baptist." Så, jeg sendte dem ned. Hvis han fant noen, sa jeg, "Det er fryktelig tørt der nede, ser du."
Så, hvis han fant noen som ønsket å bli neddykket i stedet for sprinklet, sa han, "Gå opp og se Bror Billy." Sa, "Nå, han vil drukne deg. Han er baptist."—men vi hadde bare et vennskap som det med hverandre.
Vel, han var en god mann. Og han sa, etter at jeg kom tilbake hit for cirka to år siden, litt over, "Billy, du reiser hele verden rundt, og så lar du din stakkars bror sitte her." Sa, "Hvorfor kommer du ikke ned og holder et møte for meg?"
Og jeg sa, "Vel, bror, når jeg kommer hjem, prøver jeg å hvile mitt sinn og slappe av etter bønnen for de syke."
Han sa, "Vel, Bror Branham," sa han, "jeg skal ikke be deg om å be for en eneste person, hvis du bare kommer ned og preker for oss en kveld."
Vel, han hadde et radioprogram, og han annonserte det på radio den morgenen, og den kvelden var menigheten overfylt, omtrent som denne, og folk sto overalt, til og med ute på gatene.

text:
And so when I made the altar call, we had the people up and down the streets with the speakers to accept Christ, and so forth. He'd taken me out… Come in that night, they held their hands down over the window and picked me up; and going out, he took me down a basement, said, "Wait till they get away, and I will put you in a room down here, and then you can get out."
And I said, "All right."
Going down the steps, he put his arm around me, said, "Billy, you forgive me for something?"
I said, "What'd you do, Brother Johnson?"
text_no:
Da jeg gjorde alterkallet, var det folk opp og ned i gatene med høyttalere for å ta imot Kristus, og så videre. Han førte meg ut... Da jeg kom inn den kvelden, holdt de hendene over vinduet og løftet meg opp. For å komme ut tok han meg ned i en kjeller og sa: "Vent til de har gått, så plasserer jeg deg i et rom her nede, og derfra kan du komme deg ut."
Jeg svarte: "Greit."
På vei ned trappene la han armen rundt meg og spurte: "Billy, kan du tilgi meg for noe?"
Jeg sa: "Hva har du gjort, Bror Johnson?"

text:
He said, "Well," he said, "nothing, but," said, "I've got a little lady here that's a Sunday school teacher." And said, "She's so awful bad condition, nervous." Said, "I'm… You don't have to see any vision," said, "just put your hands on her, offer prayer." Said, "She's been everywhere prayed for."
And I said, "Sure."
Said, "She's in a terrible shape." Said, "Ten years nearly she's been going to this psychiatrist, ten dollars a trip, in Louisville." Said, "She's having an awful time."
I said, "Certainly!" And I walked down at the bottom of the steps, and a very attractive little woman, about thirty, standing there.
She said, "How do you do, Brother Branham." Well, I was expecting to see somebody in a straight jacket, the way he was talking.
And… I said, "Are you the patient?"
She said, "Yes, sir, I am, Brother Branham."
I said, "What's the matter, sister?"
She said, "I don't know. That's what I'm wondering." She said, "I just … I just feel like I'm walking on the earth, and it's going to burst anytime, and I can't leave home. Somebody's got to go with me. I'm afraid of the steps." Said, "I got three little children," and said, "I'm in an awful shape." Said, "Will you pray for me?"
And I said, "Yes, ma'am." I put my hands over on her, and I prayed, "Dear Heavenly, help this little woman; with all my heart I pray." And I went on out. Then they dropped me off in a little while, and I went home.
text_no:
Han sa: "Vel, jeg har en liten dame her som er søndagsskolelærer." Han fortsatte: "Hun er i en forferdelig tilstand, veldig nervøs. Du trenger ikke å se noen visjon. Bare legg hendene på henne og be. Hun har blitt bedt for overalt."
Jeg svarte: "Selvfølgelig."
Han fortalte videre: "Hun er i en fryktelig tilstand. I nesten ti år har hun gått til en psykiater i Louisville, ti dollar per gang. Hun har det forferdelig."
Jeg sa: "Selvsagt!" Jeg gikk ned til bunnen av trappen, hvor en svært tiltrekkende liten kvinne, rundt tretti år, sto.
Hun sa: "Hvordan har du det, Bror Branham." Jeg hadde forventet å se en person i tvangstrøye, slik han beskrev det.
Jeg spurte: "Er det du som er pasienten?"
Hun svarte: "Ja, sir, det er jeg, Bror Branham."
Jeg spurte: "Hva er galt, søster?"
Hun sa: "Jeg vet ikke. Det er det jeg lurer på." Hun fortsatte: "Jeg føler bare at jeg går på jorden, og den kan briste når som helst. Jeg tør ikke forlate hjemmet uten at noen følger meg. Jeg er redd for trappene. Jeg har tre små barn, og jeg er i en forferdelig tilstand. Kan du be for meg?"
Jeg svarte: "Ja, selvfølgelig." Jeg la hendene mine på henne og ba: "Kjære Himmelske Far, hjelp denne lille kvinnen; med hele mitt hjerte ber jeg." Deretter gikk jeg videre. De slapp meg av etter en liten stund, og jeg dro hjem.

text: Two days after that I left for overseas. And while I was overseas, my wife said that poor little thing called about every two or three days; said, "Look. If Brother Branham ever has a meeting anywhere, or when he's home, if the Angel of the Lord ever comes near, Mrs. Branham, will you please let me be first one on the list?'"
text_no: To dager senere reiste jeg utenlands. Mens jeg var borte, sa min kone at den stakkars jenta ringte omtrent hver to-tre dager. Hun sa: "Hør. Hvis Bror Branham noen gang har et møte noe sted, eller når han er hjemme, og Herrens Engel kommer nær, kan du, fru Branham, vennligst sørge for at jeg blir den første på listen?"

text:
And said, "Well," she said, "I'll do my best, sister." Said, "All I know to do."
And she'd say, "Oh, she said, 'I'm just getting worse and worse all the time.' And she said, 'I can't go to one of the meetings.' Said, 'I don't how they'd do; they'd have to strap me in a car, to get me out.' Said, 'I…'" That's a terrible fix to be… That's the worst thing there is. That's mentally.
So, I said, "Well…" My wife said, rather, said, "Well, when he comes back…" When I come home, she'd call about every day; said, "No," and said that… So she come up. And she wanted me to pray for her.
I said, "All right." And I was going out; they was taking me out then. And so I stopped there on the sidewalk and prayed for her. She went back. A couple days later, she called my wife, she said, "Oh, Sister Branham, it'll just have to take when the anointing is on him, where he can rebuke the enemy." Said, "I'm dying." Said, "I just can't stand it any longer. These ten years," said, "I just can't hold up any longer. I'm just going to pieces."
text_no:
Hun sa: "Vel, jeg skal gjøre mitt beste, søster. Jeg gjør alt jeg kan." Og hun sa: "Jeg blir bare verre og verre hele tiden. Jeg kan ikke gå på ett av møtene. De måtte spenne meg fast i en bil for å få meg ut. Det er en forferdelig situasjon å være i, rent mentalt."
Min kone sa: "Når han kommer tilbake…" Når jeg kom hjem, ringte hun nesten hver dag og sa… Så kom hun opp og ønsket at jeg skulle be for henne.
Jeg sa: "Greit." Jeg var på vei ut, og de skulle ta meg ut. Jeg stoppet der på fortauet og ba for henne. Noen dager senere ringte hun min kone og sa: "Åh, søster Branham, det må skje når han er salvet, slik at han kan avvise fienden. Jeg holder på å dø. Jeg klarer det bare ikke lenger. Disse ti årene, jeg orker det ikke lenger. Jeg faller fra hverandre."

text:
So, poor little thing in that fix; you just imagine how she felt. Well, and one morning, there about three o'clock in the morning, He came into the room. And then after daylight come, while there was some people outside, my wife come into my room. She said, "Billy, has something happened?"
I said, "Honey, He's been here ever since early this morning."
She said, "I thought, when I come through this room, that there was something strange." And she said, "There's some people out there."
I said, "Well, honey…"
She said, "Shall I cook you a little breakfast?"
I said, "No, maybe He wants to do something, honey." I said, "Don't cook my breakfast."
She said, "Billy, can I call that little woman in New Albany?"
I said, "What little woman, honey?"
And she said, "The little woman that's been calling me, and is so nervous, down there."
And I said, "Well, it doesn't matter to me."
And so she called her. And there was a man from the Louisville Baptist church, one of the … Broadway Baptist church in Louisville, that was healed that morning with cancer on the spleen. And he's just fine, perfectly well now. He used to be a big league baseball player for the Louisville team. The doctors has give him up there in Louisville.
text_no:
Stakkars lille ting i den situasjonen; bare forestill deg hvordan hun følte seg. En morgen, rundt klokken tre, kom Han inn i rommet. Etter at det ble lyst, mens det var noen mennesker utenfor, kom min kone inn i rommet mitt. Hun sa: "Billy, har det hendt noe?"
Jeg svarte: "Kjære, Han har vært her siden tidlig i morges."
Hun sa: "Jeg syntes det var noe merkelig da jeg gikk gjennom dette rommet." Hun fortsatte: "Det er noen mennesker der ute."
Jeg sa: "Vel, kjære…"
Hun spurte: "Skal jeg lage litt frokost til deg?"
Jeg svarte: "Nei, kanskje Han vil gjøre noe, kjære. Ikke lag frokost til meg."
Hun spurte: "Billy, kan jeg ringe den lille kvinnen i New Albany?"
Jeg spurte: "Hvilken liten kvinne, kjære?"
Hun svarte: "Den lille kvinnen som har ringt meg, og som er så nervøs, der nede."
Jeg sa: "Vel, det er greit for meg."
Så ringte hun henne. Det var en mann fra Broadway Baptist Church i Louisville som ble helbredet for kreft på milten den morgenen. Nå er han helt frisk. Han var tidligere en toppspiller på baseballaget i Louisville. Legene i Louisville hadde gitt ham opp.

text:
So then, she got the little lady up, and some people brought her. I said, "I want to see her by myself." And she went over into the little den room; I put her over there so she'd be sure to be by herself. I walked in. Her name in Shane. And she was rubbing her hands like this, sitting there. I walked in. A little mother.
And I said, "How do you do, lady?"
And she got up; she said, "How do you do?"
I said, "Just be seated." There was a little stool sitting there, and I just sat down. I said, "Now, sister…
She said, "Oh, Brother Branham, is the … is the Angel of the Lord here?"
I said, "Yes, ma'am."
She said, "Oh, will you cast this evil spirit out of me?"
I said, "Now, just a minute, sister." I said, "You have to watch what you're casting out." I said, "People with those kind of things can get yourself in trouble with it."
Do you know that?
text_no:
Hun fikk den lille damen opp, og noen kom og brakte henne. Jeg sa: "Jeg vil se henne alene." Så hun gikk over til det lille rommet; Jeg satte henne der for å sikre at hun var alene. Jeg gikk inn. Hun het Shane. Hun satt der og gned hendene. Jeg gikk inn. En liten mor.
Jeg sa: "Hvordan har du det, frue?"
Hun reiste seg og sa: "Hvordan har du det?"
Jeg sa: "Bare sett deg." Det var en liten krakk der, og jeg satte meg. Jeg sa: "Nå, søster..."
Hun sa: "Å, Bror Branham, er Herren's Engel her?"
Jeg svarte: "Ja, frue."
Hun spurte: "Å, vil du drive ut denne onde ånd fra meg?"
Jeg sa: "Vent litt, søster." Jeg sa: "Du må være forsiktig med hva du driver ut." Jeg sa: "Folk med slike ting kan få seg selv i trøbbel med det."
Visste du det?

text:
How many believes Moses was a prophet? Sure he was. God trusted him, didn't He? He said, "Go down there and speak to the rock." Is that right? Not "Smite it"; "Speak to it." And there where the whole God's program, the whole Bible, was broke at one time. Moses instead of speaking to the rock, went down there and got angry with the people and smote the rock, and smote it the second time.
Said, "You rebels, must we fetch water?" And he got the water; he was a prophet; he could bring it anyhow, whether it was God's will… That wasn't God's will, was it? But he had power, he was a prophet. And he brought the water anyhow.
But God said, "Come up here, Moses. I'll deal with you now." Is that right? And wouldn't permit him to go…
text_no:
Hvor mange tror at Moses var en profet? Selvfølgelig var han det. Gud stolte på ham, gjorde Han ikke? Gud sa, "Gå dit ned og tal til klippen." Er det riktig? Ikke "Slå den"; "Tal til den." Der ble hele Guds program, hele Bibelen, brutt på en gang. Moses, i stedet for å tale til klippen, gikk ned, ble sint på folket og slo klippen, og slo den en gang til.
Han sa, "Dere opprørere, må vi hente vann?" Og han fikk vannet; han var en profet; han kunne frembringe det uansett, selv om det ikke var Guds vilje… Det var ikke Guds vilje, var det? Men han hadde kraft, han var en profet. Og han frembrakte vannet likevel.
Men Gud sa, "Kom hit opp, Moses. Jeg skal ta meg av deg nå." Er det riktig? Og Han tillot ham ikke å gå videre…

text:
How about Elijah, that young fellow, he was… Oh, he oughtn't to have felt that about it, he went baldheaded when he was young. And he was going back after Elijah being taken up, and the little children run behind him, said, "Old baldhead! Baldhead!" Making fun of the man. Well, he ought to have knowed better---looks to me like, but that prophet got angry. And he turned around and put a curse on those little children, and two she-bears killed forty-two little innocent children, just for calling the prophet "baldhead."
Well, what difference did that make? I don't think that was the nature of the Holy Spirit, but that was a angered prophet. So you have to watch what you're doing.
text_no:
Hva med Elia, den unge mannen, han burde... Å, han burde ikke ha følt det slik, han ble skallet da han var ung. Da han kom tilbake etter at Elias ble tatt opp, løp små barn etter ham og ropte: "Din gamle skalle! Skalle!" De gjorde narr av mannen. Vel, han burde ha visst bedre, etter min mening, men profeten ble sint. Han snudde seg og forbannet de små barna, og to binner drepte førtito uskyldige barn bare for å ha kalt profeten "skalle."
Hva så? Jeg tror ikke det var Den Hellige Ånds natur, men en sint profet. Så du må være forsiktig med hva du gjør.

text:
So I said, "Sister, let's talk a few minutes." I said, "Let's just take a little trip."
She said, "Oh, no, I can't take a trip."
I said, "Just a moment, mentally," just to get her mind started. And when we started talking, just a few moments, I seen a little car running like that, little one-seated black car. I said, "Was you ever in an accident, sister?"
Said, "No, no, I never was in an accident." And then, just then, here it come across the room again.
I said, "I see a little car. And, oh, a train almost hits it." And she started screaming then. And then, of course, the vision done started; there was no way of stopping it then. So she said … told her, said, "Now, you was in a little black car, and you was with a blond-headed man. And you were coming back from being out and doing wrong, and the train almost hit you." And it told her about her husband, she had just gotten married, and her husband left and went overseas. And she got lonesome, and she started running around with some boy, and she was out with him one night, and broke her marriage vows. And on the road home, they almost got hit by a train. And when the vision left, the woman was laying on the floor, just screaming the top of her voice. And my wife come running to the room. And she wanted to know what had happened.
She got the girl up, and she said, "Oh, Reverend Branham, don't you dare to tell that to nobody."
And I said, "Sister, the only thing you can do is go and confess that to your husband, and make it right." I said, "There's your trouble."
She said, "I have confessed that to God, Brother Branham."
text_no:
Så jeg sa: "Søster, la oss snakke noen minutter." Jeg sa: "La oss ta en liten reise."
Hun sa: "Å nei, jeg kan ikke reise."
Jeg sa: "Bare et øyeblikk, mentalt," for å få henne til å begynne å tenke. Og da vi begynte å snakke, etter bare noen få minutter, så jeg en liten bil kjøre slik, en liten énseters svart bil. Jeg spurte: "Har du noen gang vært i en ulykke, søster?"
Hun svarte: "Nei, nei, jeg har aldri vært i en ulykke." Og så, akkurat da, dukket det opp igjen over rommet.
Jeg sa: "Jeg ser en liten bil. Og, å, et tog nesten treffer den." Da begynte hun å skrike. Og selvfølgelig, da visjonen startet, var det ingen måte å stoppe den på. Så hun fortalte at hun hadde vært i en liten svart bil med en blond mann. De hadde vært ute og gjort noe galt, og toget nesten traff dem. Visjonen fortalte om hennes ektemann, som hun nylig hadde giftet seg med, og som hadde reist utenlands. Hun hadde følt seg ensom og begynte å være sammen med en annen mann. De var ute en natt og brøt sine ekteskapsløfter. På vei hjem holdt de på å bli truffet av et tog. Da visjonen tok slutt, lå kvinnen på gulvet og skrek. Min kone kom løpende inn i rommet for å finne ut hva som hadde skjedd.
Hun fikk kvinnen opp, og kvinnen sa: "Å, Bror Branham, ikke våg å fortelle dette til noen."
Jeg sa: "Søster, det eneste du kan gjøre er å gå og bekjenne dette for din mann og gjøre det rette." Jeg sa: "Det er ditt problem."
Hun sa: "Jeg har bekjent det for Gud, Bror Branham."

text:
I said, "It wasn't God you sinned against. You was a married woman, you sinned against your vows." And I said, "They might pour oil on your head by the gallons; they might pray, and stomp, and kick devils; it'll never make a bit of difference. You got unconfessed sin; it will stay right there till that thing is made right. I don't care how many prayers of faith, or anything else, it'll stay right there."
And she said, "Well…"
And I said, "You a Sunday school teacher?"
And she said, "Well, Brother Branham, that'll break our home up."
I said, "Now, sister, only thing I know, that's the truth. Nobody knows that but you and God alone, and that boy."
She said, "That's right."
I said, "You told your husband that you was out with a boy, but never confessed it all."
text_no:
Jeg sa, "Det var ikke Gud du syndet mot. Du var en gift kvinne; du syndet mot dine løfter." Jeg sa, "De kan helle olje på hodet ditt i bøttevis; de kan be, trampe og drive ut djevler; det vil ikke gjøre noen forskjell. Du har ubekjent synd; den vil forbli der til det er ordnet opp. Jeg bryr meg ikke om hvor mange bønner i tro eller noe annet, det vil forbli der."
Hun sa, "Vel…"
Jeg spurte, "Er du en søndagsskolelærer?"
Hun svarte, "Vel, Bror Branham, det vil ødelegge hjemmet vårt."
Jeg svarte, "Nå, søster, det eneste jeg vet, er at dette er sannheten. Ingen vet det unntatt deg og Gud alene, og den gutten."
Hun sa, "Det stemmer."
Jeg sa, "Du sa til mannen din at du var ute med en gutt, men du har aldri bekjent alt."

text:
She said, "That's right." She said, "But I can't tell him." Said, "I've got three children, Brother Branham. It'd break my home up." She said, "My husband's a deacon down there in the church." And said, "If I'd ever tell him that," said, "it'd break our home up."
I said, "Well, there…"
She said, "Well, that isn't what's bothering me."
I said, "Oh, yes, it is. It's way back in that subconscious there, still cut a streak." I said, "There's where it's at, right there. That's right."
And she said, "Well, I … I just can't tell him. It'd break my home up."
I said, "Well, only thing I can do, sister, there's what He said. You knowed whether anybody knowed about it or not, so…" Wife… I was going to the next room.
She said, "Oh, don't leave, Brother Branham."
I said, "Well, sister, you go get your husband and make that right."
She said, "I just can't do it."
text_no:
Hun sa: "Det stemmer." Hun sa videre: "Men jeg kan ikke fortelle ham det." Hun sa: "Jeg har tre barn, Bror Branham. Det vil ødelegge hjemmet mitt." Hun la til: "Min mann er diakon i menigheten." Og sa: "Hvis jeg noen gang fortalte ham det, ville det ødelegge hjemmet vårt."
Jeg sa: "Vel, det er..."
Hun avbrøt: "Vel, det er ikke det som plager meg."
Jeg sa: "Å, jo, det er det. Det ligger langt der bak i underbevisstheten din, fortsatt skaper det en splid." Jeg sa: "Der ligger problemet, akkurat der. Det er riktig."
Hun sa: "Vel, jeg... jeg kan bare ikke fortelle ham det. Det ville ødelegge hjemmet mitt."
Jeg svarte: "Vel, det eneste jeg kan gjøre, søster, er å si det Han sa. Du visste det, uansett om noen andre visste om det eller ikke, så..." Kona mi... Jeg var på vei til neste rom.
Hun ropte: "Å, ikke gå, Bror Branham."
Jeg sa: "Vel, søster, du må gå til din mann og gjøre dette opp."
Hun sa: "Jeg kan bare ikke gjøre det."

text:
I happened to look, and standing by her side was a black-headed man, kind of wavy hair, over to the side like that. And he turned his back, and he had wrote on… He had a Chevrolet sign wrote on his back. And I said, "Isn't your husband a tall man with black wavy hair?"
She said, "Yes, sir."
I said, "He works for a Chevrolet company."
Said, "Yes, sir."
I said, "He has the same thing to confess to you." That's right. I said, "He works in a garage. And day before yesterday, he was with a black-headed woman that works in the garage. She was wearing a pink dress. They was in a green Chevrolet car. And she broke his marriage vow to you. And not only that, but he's done it before."
Said, "Not my husband. He's a deacon in a church."
I said, "No wonder Brother Johnson's church is not prospering with such as that." And I said, "That's it."
And she said, "Not my husband." She resented it. She believed him. She said, "Not my husband."
I said, "Look, lady, you better go and get your husband, you all get these things straightened up, 'cause that's all I know, now. I'm just His servant." And I walked out of the room.
My wife come back in a few minutes. She said, "She went over to the phone; she called him up." Said, "The women are sitting her back down." So, said, "What do you think will happen?"
text_no:
Jeg kikket tilfeldigvis, og ved siden av henne sto en mann med svart, litt bølgete hår, som lå over til siden. Han snudde ryggen til, og jeg så at han hadde et Chevrolet-emblem på ryggen. Jeg sa, "Er ikke mannen din høy med svart bølgete hår?"
Hun svarte, "Ja, sir."
Jeg sa, "Han jobber for et Chevrolet-selskap."
Hun sa, "Ja, sir."
Jeg sa, "Han må tilstå det samme til deg. Det stemmer. Han jobber i et verksted, og dagen før i går var han sammen med en svart-håret kvinne som også jobber i verkstedet. Hun hadde på seg en rosa kjole, og de var i en grønn Chevrolet. Hun fikk ham til å bryte ekteskapsløftet sitt til deg. Og det er ikke første gang."
Hun svarte, "Ikke min mann. Han er diakon i en menighet."
Jeg sa, "Ikke rart at Bror Johnsons menighet ikke blomstrer med slikt som det." Og jeg sa, "Det er alt."
Hun protesterte, "Ikke min mann." Hun stolte på ham og gjentok, "Ikke min mann."
Jeg sa, "Hør her, frue, du bør hente mannen din og få orden på dette, for det er alt jeg vet. Jeg er bare Hans tjener." Så gikk jeg ut av rommet.
Kona mi kom tilbake etter noen minutter og sa, "Hun gikk til telefonen og ringte ham. Kvinnene setter henne ned igjen nå." Så sa hun, "Hva tror du vil skje?"

text:
I said, "Well, that's up to them; I don't know. But," I said, "you never know, no matter how much you scream, how loud you scream, how much you'd pray, that devil has got a legal right to stay right there, and he'll stay there, too. That's right. And he has got a right to stay there. Not only that, but if you're supposed to have done something and didn't do it, he has a legal right of disobedience." So, there's what it is. Combing that person, you find out just where that cause is.
Then you know where to start for the cure, see. It's unconfessed sin, many times, so you have to watch what you say, stomping this one out and stomping that one out, see. That's right, see. Faith will move. But not when he's got a legal right. Satan knows what his rights is, and what he hasn't got a right to. That's right. Clean yourself up before God and come before Him pure and holy, ask then, see. And now, search your life out.
text_no:
Jeg sa, "Vel, det er opp til dem; jeg vet ikke. Men," sa jeg, "du vet aldri, uansett hvor mye du skriker, hvor høyt du roper, hvor mye du ber; den djevelen har en juridisk rett til å bli akkurat der, og han vil bli der, også. Det er riktig. Og han har rett til å bli der. Ikke bare det, men hvis du skulle ha gjort noe og ikke gjorde det, har han en juridisk rett på grunn av ulydighet." Så, der har du det. Ved å gå gjennom den personen, finner du ut nøyaktig hvor årsaken ligger.
Da vet du hvor du skal starte for å finne kuren, ser du. Det er uomvendt synd, ofte, så du må passe på hva du sier, trampe ned denne og trampe ned den andre, ser du. Det er riktig, ser du. Tro vil bevege seg. Men ikke når han har en juridisk rett. Satan vet hva hans rettigheter er, og hva han ikke har rett til. Det er riktig. Rens deg selv for Gud og kom for Ham ren og hellig, be så, ser du. Og nå, undersøk livet ditt.

text:
Then after a while here they come back. She went and got him, and he come up the road in a car. So, they stopped and got out. And she said, "Honey, I got something to confess to you." And she told him what it was. And he said… "And you know, wasn't you out?" She knew the woman, said, "Wasn't you out?"
He said, "No!"
Said, "You was at a certain place, at a certain…
He said, "Where have you been?"
And she said, "I've been up to Brother Branham's."
He took… He said, "Honey, that's the honest truth." And he said, "I tell you what." Said, "Frankly…" Now, it told about him when he was in France, and so forth, and he said, "I started it, I'm the guilty one." Said, "I was the one who started… Said, "If you'll forgive me, I will forgive you. We'll clean ourselves up before God, and walk over there, and take our little children and live like Christians ought to." There they confessed their sins to each other and to God, and got right with God.
text_no:
Etter en stund kom de tilbake. Hun hentet ham, og han kom opp veien i bil. De stoppet og gikk ut av bilen. Hun sa: "Elskede, jeg har noe å bekjenne for deg." Hun fortalte ham hva det var. Han sa: "Visste du ikke om det?" Hun kjente kvinnen og sa: "Var ikke du ute?"
Han svarte: "Nei!"
Han sa: "Du var på et bestemt sted, på en bestemt tid..."
Han spurte: "Hvor har du vært?"
Hun svarte: "Jeg har vært hos Bror Branham."
Han sa: "Elskede, det er den rene sannhet." Så sa han: "Jeg skal fortelle deg noe. Ærlig talt..." Han fortalte om tiden i Frankrike, og fortsatte: "Det var jeg som startet det, jeg er den skyldige." Han sa: "Hvis du tilgir meg, vil jeg tilgi deg. Vi skal gjøre oss rene for Gud, ta med våre små barn og leve som kristne bør."
Der bekjente de sine synder for hverandre og for Gud, og gjorde opp sin sak med Gud.

text:
When they walked back up on the porch with their arm around one another, tears rolling down their cheeks.
I said, "Now, now, Satan will leave. Now something will take place." And the woman is perfectly normal and well from that minute on, see. There it is. Laying way back in her soul, you see. Things that you've maybe forgot about, but they're laying there, see, and so forth. My, so many things, friends. I've seen our Lord Jesus, thousands of those things right on the platform.
How many has been in the meetings and seen such as that right on the platform, where people had lived wrong, and things, and rebuked their sins. Let's see your hands, people that's been in the meetings. Sure. That's what it's for.
text_no:
Da de gikk tilbake opp på verandaen med armene rundt hverandre, og tårene rant nedover kinnene deres, sa jeg: "Nå, nå, Satan vil forlate. Nå vil noe skje." Og fra det øyeblikket var kvinnen helt normal og frisk. Der ser du. Langt tilbake i sjelen hennes lå ting du kanskje har glemt, men de er der, forstår du, og så videre. Å, så mange ting, venner. Jeg har sett vår Herre Jesus manifestere tusenvis av slike ting rett på plattformen.
Hvor mange har vært på møtene og sett slike ting rett på plattformen, hvor folk har levd feil, og har fått sine synder irettesatt? La oss se hendene deres, dere som har vært på møtene. Selvsagt. Det er det møtene er til for.

text:
Now, quickly to our text. Now, we got about ten minutes before the prayer line, fifteen.
Notice. A man, Simeon, an old sage, white beard man with long hair, was a teacher in Israel. And he was a great man, had a lot of prestige among the Jews. And the Jews was just about the condition of the church then, is just about like it is today. Kind of cold and indifferent and they had been looking for Christ to come for four thousand years. And little did they think then, when they was in the condition that they was then, that Christ would come then. But, sometimes when you least think, that's when He comes. When you think He isn't coming, that's when He will come.
And then, in this place, the old fellow went around testifying, 'cause one day the Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wasn't going to die until he had seen the Lord's Christ. And he went around telling everybody that he was going to see the Christ before he died.
Now, could you imagine what effect that had on the people? Perhaps, the scientists and the doctors and the ministers and so forth. You know what they probably said about the old man? "He had something happen in his head." Look at him nearly now, around ninety years old, and his white whiskers hanging down, old man like he is. And even David expected to see Christ. Like at him here, saying, that 'The Holy Ghost told him that he was going to see Christ before he died.' Well, that's ridiculous; it brings reproach upon our church." I can just imagine. But he had a real good right to believe it, because the Holy Spirit told him he was going to do it.
text_no:
La oss raskt gå til teksten. Vi har omtrent ti minutter før bønnekøen, femten.
Legg merke til en mann, Simeon. En gammel vismann, hvitt skjegg og langt hår, en lærer i Israel. Han var en betydelig mann med høy anseelse blant jødene. Men menigheten var omtrent i samme tilstand som den er i dag: litt kald og likegyldig. De hadde ventet på Kristi komme i fire tusen år. Da, som nå, forventet de knapt at Kristus ville komme, spesielt ikke i den tilstanden de var i. Men ofte kommer Han når man minst venter det. Når du tror Han ikke kommer, er det da Han viser seg.
Denne gamle mannen gikk rundt og vitnet, for én dag hadde Den Hellige Ånd åpenbart for ham at han ikke skulle dø før han hadde sett Herrens Kristus. Han fortalte alle at han skulle se Kristus før han døde.
Kan du forestille deg hvilken effekt dette hadde på folk? Kanskje forskere, leger og predikanter og så videre. Du vet hva de sannsynligvis sa om den gamle mannen: "Det er noe galt med hodet hans." Se på ham, nesten nitti år gammel med hvitt skjegg hengende ned. Selv David forventet å se Kristus. Her går han rundt og sier: "Den Hellige Ånd fortalte meg at jeg skal se Kristus før jeg dør." Vel, det er latterlig; det bringer skam over vår menighet." Jeg kan bare forestille meg. Men Simeon hadde god grunn til å tro det, fordi Den Hellige Ånd hadde fortalt ham det.

text:
Now, we don't have two Holy Spirits; we only have one. The same Holy Spirit that was on Simeon is the same Holy Spirit that's here tonight. Men go; the Holy Spirit remains just the same.
Notice. Then, we're going to use the word of expectations. Usually you get what you expect to. If you come to church tonight, just saying, "I'm going over there, I'm going over there to find something that I know that's wrong." Don't worry; the devil will show you plenty of it. That's right. You usually get what you expect. If you come expecting to get a blessing from God, God will see that you get it. What you expect! You say, "Well, I don't expect it to be." Well, that's the reason it isn't. You've got to believe it. And you believe it to be right. Look at the right things, examine it right.
Now, Simeon went to … was expecting that he was going to see the Christ. Don't make any difference how much people said he was crazy, that didn't bother him one bit. He believed that he was going to see the Christ. So, when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea, they didn't have television and radio and press like they have today. And probably if they would had it, they wouldn't use it for that. But, you know God has a way of getting His message out anyhow. Don't you think so?
text_no:
Vi har ikke to Hellige Ånder; vi har bare én. Den samme Hellige Ånd som var over Simeon, er den samme Hellige Ånd som er her i kveld. Mennesker kommer og går; den Hellige Ånd forblir den samme.
Legg merke til dette. Nå skal vi bruke ordet om forventninger. Vanligvis får du det du forventer. Hvis du kom til menigheten i kveld og bare sier, "Jeg drar dit for å finne noe jeg vet er galt," ikke bekymre deg; djevelen vil vise deg mye av det. Det stemmer. Du får vanligvis det du forventer. Hvis du kommer med forventning om å få en velsignelse fra Gud, vil Gud sørge for at du får det. Det handler om hva du forventer! Du sier: "Vel, jeg forventer ikke at det skal være slik." Vel, det er grunnen til at det ikke skjer. Du må tro det. Og du må tro at det er rett. Se på de rette tingene, undersøk dem nøye.
Simeon forventet at han skulle få se Kristus. Det spilte ingen rolle hvor mye folk sa han var gal; det plaget ham ikke det minste. Han trodde han skulle få se Kristus. Da Jesus ble født i Betlehem i Judea, fantes det verken fjernsyn, radio eller presse som i dag. Og sannsynligvis ville de ikke ha brukt det til det formålet om de hadde hatt det. Men du vet, Gud har en måte å få Sin melding ut på uansett. Ikke sant?

text:
So, when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea, three magis, star gazers, came from the East. They said, "We have seen His star in the West." They were in the East, look, the Orients are east of Jerusalem. So said, "We have seen His star in the East, and have come to worship Him."
Look. Do you believe they actually followed a star? The Bible said they did. Now, it come over every… They kept time then by stars. And no observatory, no scientist, all the people standing up on the watches, looking at every star moving, to see what time it was in the night, and so forth. Star gazers. There wasn't anyone seen that Angel … or, that star but these Magis, for they was expecting to see it. They had read God's prophecy where He spoke through Balaam back there, and said, "There would be a star out of Jacob rise," and they were looking for it.
So, that's the reason, tonight, that people---the American people ---are not expecting. They're not looking for a great Holy Ghost pouring out revival. And that's the reason they're not receiving it, for it's poured out; they're not expecting it. That's all. They're looking to see a reformation in the church, and the people come back and join church and live on out in the world. It's not that age. It's the time where God is filling His people, and baptizing them into the body of the Lord Jesus Christ. And that's the reason they're not expecting it, they're not seeing it---it's going on.
text_no:
Da Jesus ble født i Betlehem i Judea, kom tre vismenn, stjernekikkere, fra Østen. De sa: «Vi har sett Hans stjerne i Østen og er kommet for å tilbe Ham.» Østliglandene ligger øst for Jerusalem. De sa: «Vi har sett Hans stjerne i Østen og har kommet for å tilbe Ham.»
Tror du de faktisk fulgte en stjerne? Bibelen sier at de gjorde det. Den viste seg overalt... De brukte stjerner til å holde tid. Ingen observatorium, ingen vitenskapsmenn, alle mennesker som sto oppe om natten og observerte hver stjerne som flyttet seg for å vite hva klokken var, og så videre. Stjernekikkere. Ingen så den stjernen, bortsett fra disse vismennene, fordi de forventet å se den. De hadde lest Guds profeti der Han talte gjennom Bileam og forutsa: «En stjerne skal stige fram fra Jakob,» og de lette etter den.
Det er grunnen til at folk i Amerika i kveld ikke forventer en stor utgytelse av Den Hellige Ånd. De mottar den ikke fordi de ikke forventer den, selv om den er utøst. De ser etter en reformasjon i kirken, hvor folk vender tilbake og går inn i menigheten, men fortsetter å leve i verden. Dette er ikke den tiden. Det er tiden hvor Gud fyller Sitt folk og døper dem inn i Herren Jesus Kristi kropp. Derfor ser de det ikke, og de forventer det ikke, selv om det skjer.

text:
So, is it possible, like in the meetings… A lady here in the meeting the other night, seen that light where it was standing here. Why, said, "The rest of them didn't see it." Certainly she could see it and the rest of them not. My! Many times.
And so then, he was expecting to see the Lord Jesus. So, the Magis was expecting to see it. And there was some shepherds out there on the hill that saw the Angels come … or, heard them, and they sang, "Glory to God in the highest, peace on earth, good will towards men." And then He was born in a manager that night. Eight days afterwards, it was the Jewish custom to take the male child to the temple and have it circumcised. And the mother had to offer an offering for her purification. That's the Levitical law. After eight days, she comes up to the temple.
text_no:
I møtene her om kvelden var det en dame som så Lyset hvor det sto. Hvorfor, sa hun, kunne ikke de andre se det? Selvfølgelig kunne hun se det, selv om de andre ikke gjorde det. Dette har skjedd mange ganger.
Han ventet på å se Herren Jesus. Magiene ventet også på å se Ham. Det var noen hyrder ute på marken som så eller hørte englene komme og synge: "Ære være Gud i det høyeste, og fred på jorden, blant mennesker Guds velbehag." Deretter ble Han født i en krybbe den natten. Åtte dager senere, ifølge jødisk skikk, skulle guttebarnet føres til tempelet for å bli omskåret. Moren måtte også gi et offer for sin renselse, etter den levittiske loven. Så, etter åtte dager, kom hun til tempelet.

text: I can imagine it being Monday morning. And there a great host of people… There was about two million people in Israel then … or, in Palestine. And I suppose to say, at least fifty to a hundred babies would be born every night. And that made fifty to a hundred babies, maybe two or three hundred of them, each day had to come and be circumcised and purification for the mother.
text_no: Jeg kan forestille meg at det er mandag morgen. En stor folkemengde er samlet... Det var omtrent to millioner mennesker i Israel da ... eller i Palestina. Jeg antar at minst femti til hundre babyer ble født hver natt. Dette førte til at femti til hundre babyer, kanskje to eller tre hundre hver dag, måtte komme for å bli omskåret og for morens renselse.

text:
And I can look at it being Monday morning. And oh, it's busy around the temple, lots of people coming and going. And way down over on the side, stands a long line, like a prayer line of women standing there with their little babies in their arms. Coming, some of them, rich babies, had lambs to offer. A peasant's offering was two turtledoves. And so, I can look down along the line; I can see the mother standing there with her fine little babies, clean and neat, and little needlework done, you know, around the babies, and so forth. I look way down the line, and I see a little woman standing down there, that all the rest of the women kept their distance from her.
That's about the way they do the church today, that believes in the supernat… They keep their distance.
"Look. Don't have nothing to do with them people. They're not right, they're holy rollers. Don't fool with them." I've sailed the seven seas, traveled around the world three times, and I've never seen a holy-roller church yet. Never seen one yet. Just a false name the devil tacked on the church of the living God. There's nine hundred and sixty-nine different churches, I have statistics of those churches, that comes from the government each year and there's not one of them called Holy-rollers, not one of them. So, it's just a scandal name the devil put on them.
text_no:
Det er mandag morgen, og det er travelt rundt tempelet. Mange mennesker kommer og går. Langs siden står en lang kø, som en bønnekø av kvinner med små babyer i armene. Noen av dem har rike babyer og har med lam som offer. En fattigmanns offer var to turtelduer. Jeg ser nedover linjen og ser mødre med sine fine, små babyer, rene og pene, med broderier rundt dem. Lenger ned ser jeg en liten kvinne som de andre kvinnene holder avstand fra.
Det er omtrent slik menigheten blir behandlet i dag, de som tror på det overnaturlige. Folk holder avstand fra dem.
"Se der. Ikke ha noe å gjøre med dem. De er ikke riktig. De er helligrullere. Ikke bry dere med dem." Jeg har seilt på de syv hav, reist verden rundt tre ganger, og jeg har aldri møtt en menighet som kalles "helligrullere". Ikke en eneste. Det er bare et falskt navn djevelen har festet på Guds levende menighet. Det finnes ni hundre og sekstini forskjellige menigheter. Jeg har statistikk fra myndighetene som oppdateres hvert år, og ingen av dem kalles "helligrullere". Ikke én. Så det er bare et skandale-navn djevelen har satt på dem.

text:
So, I can see those women with their nice little babies all in these nice needle works and so forth, and little pink gowns and so forth, standing off, holding their lambs, and so forth, walking up as the priest was doing the ministering at the altar. And this little girl, she stood out by herself, nobody around her.
I can hear some of them saying, "Look. You see her? Yeah, that baby was born out of holy wedlock. That fellow Joseph, they're not even married. Look at them there. And that baby was born, look at it there. That's nothing but disgrace. Keep your distance. That poor little mother, with that little baby in her arms, like this, little veil over her face, maybe, rocking little fellow as He gooed, like that."
What was He dressed in? Swaddling cloth. Not fine needlework; swaddling cloth. Where was He born? In a manger. Why was He born in a manger? Because He was a lamb. Lambs are not born in houses; they're born in mangers, out in straw. He was the Lamb of God, slain from the foundation of the world. And here He was. You what swaddling cloth is? It's what they take off the back of a yoke of an ox. In the Orients I learned that, that where they plow an ox. They wrap some rags around to keep from rubbing his shoulders. And in the stalls that night there was some swaddling cloth; they didn't even have nothing to put on. We're not worthy of the clothes we got on tonight. When they unwrapped this cloth off the ox neck … the yoke of the ox and wrapped it around the Saviour of the world.
text_no:
Jeg kan se for meg disse kvinnene med sine små, pene babyer, alle kledd i fine broderier og små rosa kjoler, stående i nærheten mens de holder sine lam, og så videre, mens presten utførte tjenesten ved alteret. Og denne lille jenta, hun sto for seg selv, uten noen rundt seg.
