Jeg kan høre noen av dem si: "Se på henne. Ser du henne? Ja, den babyen ble født utenfor ekteskap. Den mannen, Joseph, de er ikke engang gift. Se på dem der. Den babyen ble født i skam. Hold avstand. Stakkars lille mor, med det lille barnet i armene, som dette, med et lite slør over ansiktet, kanskje, gynget den lille, mens Han pludret, slik."
Hva var Han kledd i? Svøpeklut. Ikke fine broderier; svøpeklut. Hvor ble Han født? I en krybbe. Hvorfor ble Han født i en krybbe? Fordi Han var et lam. Lam er ikke født i hus; de er født i krybber, ute på strå. Han var Guds Lam, slaktet fra verdens grunnvoll ble lagt. Og her var Han. Vet du hva svøpeklut er? Det er det de tar av ryggen på en åks okse. I Orienten lærte jeg at når de pløyer med en okse, vikler de noen filler rundt for å unngå å gni skuldrene. Og i båsen den natten var det noen svøpekluter; de hadde ikke engang noe annet å kle Ham i. Vi er ikke verdige klærne vi har på oss i kveld. Da de viklet denne kluten av oksens åk og viklet den rundt Verdens Frelser.

text:
And here He was, the little mother rocking Him, no clothes to put on the little fellow. He humbled Himself in the humility of His flesh. And there she had Him in her arms, rocking the little fellow like this, and she come up. What kind of an offering? Two little turtledoves, a peasant, poor; yet, He owned the whole heavens and earth. He was made poor that we through His poverty might be made rich.
There she was rocking Him in her arm. No matter what they said, in her little heart, she knew where that baby come from. That's the way with every divine believer today, when they call them holy-rollers and fanatics, they know what's in their heart. They know what's taken place. They say, "Oh, you believe in that old stuff, divine healing?" Don't you worry. You might call them everything you want to, but they know where they're at. They know what they got cradled right here. That's right.
Something… The peace that passes all understanding. No matter what the world has got to say about it. Praise God. If Jesus Christ could condescend Himself, and wrapped in swaddling cloth, stand there, be made fun of, His little mother standing like that, it's good enough for me. Amen. That's all right. I don't care what the world says. In your heart, you know what it is.
text_no:
Der var hun, den lille moren som vugget Ham, uten klær å sette på den lille gutten. Han ydmyket Seg selv gjennom kjøttets ydmykhet. Der hadde hun Ham i armene, vuggende den lille gutten slik, og hun kom frem. Hva slags offer? To små turtelduer, en simpel bonde, fattig; og likevel eide Han hele himmelen og jorden. Han ble gjort fattig for at vi gjennom Hans fattigdom kunne bli rike.
Der var hun, vuggende Ham i armene sine. Uansett hva de sa, visste hun i sitt hjerte hvor den babyen kom fra. Slik er det med enhver gudfryktig troende i dag; når de blir kalt hellig-rullere og fanatikere, vet de hva som er i deres hjerte. De vet hva som har skjedd. De sier: "Å, tror du på de gamle greiene, guddommelig helbredelse?" Ikke bekymre deg. Uansett hva de kaller deg, vet de hvor de står. De vet hva de har i vuggen der. 
Noe... Freden som overgår all forstand. Uansett hva verden måtte si om det. Pris Gud. Hvis Jesus Kristus kunne ydmyke Seg selv, svøpt i enkle kluter, bli gjort narr av, mens Hans lille mor sto der slik, er det godt nok for meg. Amen. Det er greit. Jeg bryr meg ikke om hva verden sier. I hjertet ditt vet du hva det er.

text:
Notice. I can see her rocking Him. The first thing you know, I can see a mother saying, "Don't get around that woman, bypass her around, put her in the back," see. And there, her standing there rocking the little fellow, and Him looking up, his little starry-looking eyes, looking to the mother. The very God of Heaven, Emmanuel, wrapped in that little swaddling cloth there. She just rocked Him in her arms, with a piece of goods from off of the ox's yoke wrapped around Him.
No doubt, poor little … the woman looked up, and seen them other mothers. You know how mothers are about their babies? Looked up and seen them nice little babies all laying neatly, little gowns and little beds, and things laying like that. And here she was holding this. But in her heart, she knew who it was.
And then, look. That looked like a disgraceful sight to the Lord. But, wait there's something going on all this time. Back over in a prayer room, somewhere, I can see old Simeon, sitting, reading in the Scripture: "All we like sheep have gone astray. The Lord hath laid upon Him the iniquity of us all."
I can hear Simeon say, "I wonder who that means? I've been called everything, fanatic, holy-roller. Said I was gone crazy. The Holy Ghost told me one day, I was going to see Him when He come. And I believe it. 'He was wounded for our transgressions. He was bruised for our iniquity, the chastisement of our peace was upon Him.'" He was reading the scroll.
I can see him let the scroll together and stick it down in the sack like that. Look. If God made Simeon a promise to see the Saviour, God's obligated to see that he sees Him. That's right. If the Holy Ghost made the promise, the Holy Ghost has got to see that promise is carried out. If the Holy Ghost made a promise in here, "I'm the Lord that healeth thee," He's got to carry that promise out to the letter. That's right. He has got to.
text_no:
Legg merke til dette. Jeg kan se henne vugge Ham. Før du vet ordet av det, kan jeg se en mor som sier: "Hold dere unna den kvinnen, sett henne bakerst," ser du. Der står hun og vugger den lille, og Han ser opp, med sine stjerneklare øyne, på moren. Selveste Gud i Himmelen, Emmanuel, pakket inn i en liten svøp. Hun vugger Ham i armene sine, med et stykke tøy fra oksens åk rundt Ham.
Uten tvil, stakkars lille kvinne, ser opp og ser de andre mødrene. Du vet hvordan mødre er med sine babyer? Ser opp og ser de fine babyene, alle pent kledd, små kjoler og senger, og ting som ligger så ordentlig. Og her holder hun denne lille. Men i sitt hjerte, visste hun hvem det var.
Og så, se. Det så ut som om Herren var i en vanærende situasjon. Men, vent, det skjer noe hele tiden. Borte i et bønnerom, et sted, kan jeg se gamle Simeon, sittende og lese i Skriften: "Vi for alle vil som får, gå vill. Herren har lagt på Ham alles misgjerning."
Jeg kan høre Simeon si: "Jeg lurer på hvem det betyr? Jeg har blitt kalt alt mulig, fanatiker, hellig-ruller. De sa jeg var blitt gal. Den Hellige Ånd sa en dag at jeg skulle få se Ham når Han kom. Og jeg tror det. 'Han ble såret for våre overtredelser. Han ble knust for våre misgjerninger, straffen for vår fred var på Ham.'" Han leste pergamentrullen.
Jeg kan se ham rulle sammen pergamentet og legge det ned i sekken. Se. Hvis Gud ga Simeon et løfte om å se Frelseren, er Gud forpliktet til å sørge for at han ser Ham. Det er riktig. Dersom Den Hellige Ånd ga løftet, må Den Hellige Ånd sikre at løftet oppfylles. Hvis Den Hellige Ånd ga et løfte her, "Jeg er Herren som helbreder deg," må Han oppfylle det løftet til punkt og prikke. Det er riktig. Han må.

text:
How many in here tonight believe in divine healing? Let's see your hands. What makes you believe in divine healing? David said, "When the deep calleth to the deep." If there's a deep in here calling, there's got to be a deep to respond to it somewhere. How many times have I stood in the mountains and watched the rainbows, and watched the eagle when he soars into the air; and would cry like a baby. Why? I'm a lover of nature. When I see those things…
A while ago, we was at the room where we're at, and they was catching brother's program here, coming in. It was a lovely program, tonight. And I appreciate the song, whatever it was: Down From His Glory. Brother dedicated to me. And we was sitting there talking, in a few moments, I was just rubbing my eyes like that, and a program come on in a few minutes, down there. We hadn't turned the radio off yet. An old timber wolf got to howling. You talk about crying, I did. See, there's something in that. "A deep calleth to the deep." In other words, here, before there was a fin on a fish's back, there had to be a water for him to swim in first, or he wouldn't've had no fin. Is that right? Before there's a tree to grow in the earth, or a flower to grow in the earth, there had to be an earth first, or there wouldn't be no flower to grow. Is that right?
text_no:
Hvor mange her i kveld tror på guddommelig helbredelse? La meg se hendene deres. Hva får dere til å tro på guddommelig helbredelse? David sa: "Når dyp kaller på dyp." Hvis det er et dyp her som kaller, må det være et dyp et eller annet sted som kan svare på det. Hvor mange ganger har jeg stått i fjellene og sett regnbuene, og sett ørnen sveve i luften, og grått som et barn. Hvorfor? Fordi jeg elsker naturen. Når jeg ser slike ting…
For en stund siden var vi på rommet hvor vi er nå, og de fanget inn Brors program her. Det var et nydelig program i kveld. Jeg satte pris på sangen, uansett hvilken det var: Down From His Glory. Bror dedikerte den til meg. Vi satt der og snakket, og i løpet av noen minutter, mens jeg gned meg i øynene, kom et program på om noen minutter. Vi hadde ikke slått av radioen ennå. En gammel tømmerulv begynte å hyle. For ikke å snakke om gråt, jeg gråt virkelig. Ser dere, det er noe i det der. "Et dyp kaller på et dyp." Med andre ord, før det var en finne på en fiskerygg, måtte det være vann først for at fisken skulle kunne svømme, ellers ville den ikke hatt noen finne. Er det riktig? Før det kunne vokse et tre i jorden, eller en blomst i jorden, måtte jorden først eksistere, ellers ville det ikke vært noen blomst som kunne vokse. Er det riktig?

text: Here some time ago, I read where a little boy, little baby boy, eat the erasers off of pencils. And he eat the rubber petal off of a bicycle. And they taken him to a clinic to find out what was the matter with the little fellow. And they examined him and found out his little body needed sulfur. There was something calling here for sulfur. And sulfur's in rubber. Now look. Before there could be a call in here for sulfur, there has to be a sulfur to respond to that call. You get what I mean? And as long as you believe in divine healing, there's got to be a fountain open somewhere of divine healing that calls. Yes, sir.
text_no: For en tid tilbake leste jeg om en liten gutt, en spedbarnsgutt, som spiste viskelærene på blyanter og gummipedalen på en sykkel. De tok ham med til en klinikk for å finne ut hva som feilet ham. Etter undersøkelser fant de ut at kroppen hans trengte svovel. Det var noe i kroppen som ropte etter svovel, og svovel finnes i gummi. Se her. Før det kan være et behov for svovel, må det finnes svovel et sted som kan tilfredsstille det behovet. Forstår dere hva jeg mener? Så lenge du tror på guddommelig helbredelse, må det finnes en kilde for guddommelig helbredelse et eller annet sted som svarer på det kallet. Ja, sir.

text:
The Indian… When we first come over here, in these New England states here, our forefathers, they found the Indian. They worshipped the sun, they worshipped something. Why? They were human beings, see. And they knowed there was a deep calling somewhere. They didn't know where it was at, but they were calling for it.
And Hottentots in Africa: I meet them packing little mud idols sprinkled in blood. They know there's a God; they just don't know who He is. And they worship something, because there's a deep calling to a deep. And if there's a deep calling, there's got to be a deep to respond to it. The Holy Ghost had promised Simeon that he'd see the Master. And here the Master was in the temple at that time. Little did anybody know it but Mary.
text_no:
Da våre forfedre først kom til de nordøstlige delstatene, møtte de indianerne. Indianerne tilbad solen; de tilbad noe. Hvorfor? Fordi de var mennesker og visste at det var noe dypt inni dem som kalte på noe. De visste ikke hva det var, men de følte kallet.
Hottentotter i Afrika har jeg møtt som bærer rundt på små leireidoler besprengt med blod. De vet at det finnes en Gud; de vet bare ikke hvem Han er. Og de tilber noe, fordi det er en dyp lengsel i dem. Og hvis det finnes en dyp lengsel, må det også finnes noe dypt som kan svare på den.
Den Hellige Ånd hadde lovet Simeon at han skulle se Mesteren. Og Mesteren var i tempelet på den tiden, selv om kun Maria visste det.

text:
And here he was sitting back there, reading these scrolls. I can see Him when He was sitting there. You believe we're led by the Spirit of God? Sons of God are led by the Spirit of God. I can see Simeon sitting back there reading the scroll. All at once, the Holy Spirit come, said, "Simeon!"
He stuck the scroll down in the box, raised up and said, "Yes, my Lord." Oh, my! The very promise that He had give him was in the building at that time.
He said, "Get up and walk." I can see Simeon, don't know where he's going, but he's led by the Holy Spirit." You believe we're led by the Holy Spirit?
Here he comes. He don't know where he's going, he don't know where his next move is, he's just walking, led by the Holy Spirit.
I can see him walk out into the temple, thousands going in and out. I can see him go over to this line, where these women are. Holy Ghost leading right down this line. Here he comes, right down. Stops in front of this little disgraceful-looking woman, standing there, that they called her---disgrace. Picked up that little treasure in swaddling cloth, tears running down his white beard, placed Him up like this, and said, "Lord, let Your servant depart in peace according to Your Word, for mine eyes have seen Your salvation." No matter how disgraceful it looked to the rest of them, the Holy Ghost led him straight to the Saviour.
text_no:
Der satt han, lesende disse skriftene. Jeg kan se Ham der. Tror dere at vi blir ledet av Guds Ånd? Guds sønner blir ledet av Guds Ånd. Jeg kan se Simeon sittende der og lese skriftene. Plutselig kommer Den Hellige Ånd og sier: "Simeon!" Han la skriften ned i boksen, reiste seg og sa: "Ja, min Herre." Å, min! Det løftet Han hadde gitt ham var i bygningen på det tidspunktet.
Han sa: "Reis deg og gå." Jeg kan se Simeon, som ikke vet hvor han er på vei, men han er ledet av Den Hellige Ånd. Tror dere at vi blir ledet av Den Hellige Ånd? Her kommer han. Han vet ikke hvor han går, han vet ikke hva hans neste steg er, han bare går, ledet av Den Hellige Ånd.
Jeg kan se ham gå ut i templet, tusenvis av mennesker går inn og ut. Jeg kan se ham gå til den rekken hvor disse kvinnene er. Den Hellige Ånd leder ham rett ned denne rekken. Her kommer han, rett ned. Stopper foran denne lille, vanærende kvinnen, som de kalte henne - vanæren. Han løftet opp den lille skatten i svøpte klær, tårer renner nedover hans hvite skjegg, løftet Ham opp slik og sa: "Herre, la Din tjener fare herfra i fred etter Ditt Ord, for mine øyne har sett Din frelse." Uansett hvor vanærende det så ut for de andre, ledet Den Hellige Ånd ham rett til Frelseren.

text: In the same time, there was an old blind prophetess sitting over there by the name of Anna. We've said she was blind, about eighty years old. And there she was sitting over there. She was watching for the coming of the Lord, and the same time, the Holy Ghost struck her. And here she come, blind, moving through that crowd. Nobody told her. She was led by the Holy Ghost, through that crowd. Moving around the crowd. The blind woman, and come over, a prophet. Standing there by the side of the child lifted up her hands and blessed God. If the Holy Ghost could lead that blind woman so around those people to come to the promise that God had made, the same Holy Ghost that led Simeon and led Anna by a promise of God, has led you here tonight.
text_no: Samtidig satt det en eldre, blind profetinne ved navn Anna der. Hun var omtrent åtti år gammel. Der satt hun, ventende på Herrens komme, da Den Hellige Ånd rørte ved henne. Blind som hun var, begynte hun å bevege seg gjennom folkemengden. Ingen fortalte henne det; hun ble ledet av Den Hellige Ånd gjennom mengden. Den blinde kvinnen kom fram til et barn, løftet hendene sine og velsignet Gud. Dersom Den Hellige Ånd kunne lede denne blinde kvinnen gjennom folkemengden til å komme til det løftet Gud hadde gitt, så kan den samme Den Hellige Ånd som ledet Simeon og Anna ved Guds løfte, også ha ledet deg hit i kveld.

text:
God's under obligation.
If you believe in divine healing to lead you to the fountain that's open… Don't you believe that? Yes, sir. If your hearts are calling, the deep is calling to the deep, there has got to be a deep to respond to it. You believe it, and God has led you here for that purpose tonight. You wouldn't have come out on a slick snowy night like this just to show what kind of clothes you were wearing. You never come here for no other purpose because you believed that God was the healer.
And as long as that's in your bosom, God's under obligation to place it before you, 'cause you're calling for it. And God will place it before you, then it's up to you to whatever you want to do about it. Simeon could have seen Him and walked away from it. But he embraced it.
And tonight, when the Holy Spirit begins to move through the building, in the prayer line, reach out and embrace God's promise. It's for you. It doesn't matter whether you get up here or not. That doesn't have nothing to do with it. Up here, that doesn't have anything. You just embrace it, right where you're at, and God's under obligation to bring it to you. Don't you believe that?
Led by the Spirit of God. How that Spirit-filled lives are always led by the Spirit of God. Certainly! I'm going to let you in on just a little something now, if you'll just excuse me for another few seconds here … a few minutes. I want to say something just now. You believe in being led by the Spirit of God?
text_no:
Gud er forpliktet.
Hvis du tror på guddommelig helbredelse for å lede deg til den åpne kilden... Tror du ikke det? Ja, det gjør du. Hvis ditt hjerte kaller, dybden kaller til dybden, må det være en dybde som svarer på det. Du tror det, og Gud har ledet deg hit for dette formålet i kveld. Du ville ikke ha kommet ut på en glatt, snødekket natt som denne bare for å vise hva slags klær du har på deg. Du kom ikke hit for noe annet formål fordi du trodde at Gud var helbrederen.
Og så lenge denne troen er i ditt hjertets dyp, er Gud forpliktet til å legge det fram for deg, for du kaller på det. Og Gud vil legge det fram for deg, så er det opp til deg hva du vil gjøre med det. Simeon kunne ha sett Ham og gått bort fra Ham. Men han omfavnet Ham.
Og i kveld, når Den Hellige Ånd begynner å bevege seg gjennom bygningen, i bønnekøen, rekke ut og omfavne Guds løfte. Det er for deg. Det spiller ingen rolle om du kommer opp her eller ikke. Det har ingenting å gjøre med det. Her oppe, det har ingenting å gjøre med det. Bare omfavn det akkurat der du er, og Gud er forpliktet til å gi det til deg. Tror du ikke det?
Ledet av Guds Ånd. Hvordan Åndsfylte liv alltid ledes av Guds Ånd. Selvfølgelig! Jeg skal la deg få vite en liten ting nå, hvis du bare unnskylder meg i noen få sekunder her... noen minutter. Jeg vil bare si noe nå. Tror du på å bli ledet av Guds Ånd?

text: Here sometime ago, I was in Arkansas, and I was coming out, just when I first started out in my meetings. And I was coming out of a place, where they was bringing me out of an auditorium, at Camden, Arkansas, and the police was bringing me out. And there was the people crying, trying to run through and touch your clothes. You know how people are. And I got a heart, the same as anybody else has. And I was going along there, and I heard somebody hollering, "Mercy! Mercy!" And I looked over to the side, and there stood an old colored man. They have segregation there, you know. Just a little white rim of hair around his head, holding his cap like this, he saying, "Mercy! Mercy!"
text_no: For en tid tilbake var jeg i Arkansas, akkurat i starten av mine møter. Jeg kom ut av et auditorium i Camden, Arkansas, og politiet fulgte meg ut. Menneskene ropte og forsøkte å røre ved klærne mine. Du vet hvordan folk kan være. Jeg har et hjerte som alle andre. Mens jeg gikk der hørte jeg noen rope: "Nåde! Nåde!" Jeg så til siden og der sto en gammel mørkhudet mann. De har segregasjon der, vet du. Han hadde en tynn hvit hårkant rundt hodet og holdt capsen sin i hånden mens han ropte: "Nåde! Nåde!"

text:
The Holy Spirit said to me, "Go over to him."
Well, I said to the police, I said, "I shall go over."
Said, "Rev. Branham, you can't do that. You're in the South." Said, "You go over to that colored man," said, "you'll start a race riot, as sure as the world." Said, "These white people wouldn't stand for it."
I said, "I don't care what they think, God Almighty tells me to go to that man." And I started towards him. They run over there, and pulled a line around.
And I heard his wife say to him, said, "Honey, here comes the parson."
I went over there. I said, "What do you want, Uncle?" And they formed a ring around like that to keep the people back, and people screaming and going on, you know.
And she said, "Here comes the parson." I walked up to him---still the anointing… They was just bringing me out of the meeting.
And so, then, he come over, said … feeling like that, said, "Is this you, parson?" And he felt around my face.
I said, "Yes, sir."
He said, "Parson, is your name Branham?"
And I said, "Yes, sir."
text_no:
Den Hellige Ånd sa til meg: "Gå bort til ham." 
Jeg sa til politiet: "Jeg skal gå bort." 
De sa: "Pastor Branham, du kan ikke gjøre det. Du er i Sørstatene. Hvis du går bort til den fargede mannen, vil du starte et raseopprør, helt sikkert. Disse hvite folkene vil ikke tåle det."
Jeg svarte: "Jeg bryr meg ikke om hva de tenker; Gud den Allmektige sier at jeg skal gå til den mannen." Jeg begynte å gå mot ham. Politiet løp bort og dannet en linje rundt ham.
Jeg hørte kona hans si: "Kjære, her kommer pastoren." 
Jeg gikk bort og spurte: "Hva ønsker du, onkel?" De dannet en ring rundt for å holde folk tilbake, og folk ropte og skrek.
Kona hans sa igjen: "Her kommer pastoren." Jeg gikk opp til ham, fremdeles under salvelsen, mens de nettopp hadde tatt meg ut av møtet.
Han kom bort og kjente rundt ansiktet mitt og sa: "Er det deg, pastor?" 
Jeg svarte: "Ja, sir."
Han spurte: "Pastor, er navnet ditt Branham?"
Jeg svarte: "Ja, sir."

text: And he said, "Parson Branham, I never heard of you in my life, but," said, "my old mammy had the same kind of 'ligion you got." 'Ligion, you know, said, "Same kind of 'ligion you got." Said, "Her never told me a lie, all her life, or no one else." Said, "I's been blind for many years, parson." And said, "I live about two hundred miles from here. And last night, in the night, I woke up." And said, "Just as clear as I ever seen in my life, there stood my old mammy, standing by the side of the bed. She said, 'Honey child, you raise up in here, and put on your clothes. And you go down to Camden, Arkansas, and ask for a man named Branham. Tell him to pray for you. You'll get your sight.'"
text_no: Han sa: "Pastor Branham, jeg har aldri hørt om deg før, men," sa han, "min gamle mor hadde den samme slags religion som du har." Religion, forstår du, sa han, "Samme slags religion som du har." Han sa: "Hun løy aldri for meg eller noen andre hele sitt liv." Han sa: "Jeg har vært blind i mange år, pastor." Og han sa: "Jeg bor omtrent to hundre mil herfra, og i natt våknet jeg." Han sa: "Så klart som jeg noen gang har sett i mitt liv, sto min gamle mor ved siden av sengen. Hun sa: 'Kjære, reis deg og ta på deg klærne. Dra til Camden, Arkansas, og spør etter en mann ved navn Branham. Be ham be for deg, så vil du få synet tilbake.'"

text:
Oh-h-h-h-h! What a feeling! I didn't know nothing to do. I put my hand on the old fellow, I said, "God, I don't understand this, Your Spirit led me over here, it's led him here, we know not one another; but, if Thou hast sent him, Lord, then You'll, surely, You'll confirm Your Word."
I took my hand off of him, like that, and they was pushing, you know, trying to break that line, them police holding them back like that, to go through. I took my hands down like that, started back. And he said, "Thank You, Lord. Thank You, Lord."
And his wife said, "You see, honey?"
He said, "Sure!"
Said, "Oh! You really see, honey?"
Said, "Sure!" Said, "See that red car sitting there---going to take Parson Branham to?" Everybody begin to scream. There it was.
Oh, here about two or three years ago, I heard from him. He's preaching the Gospel, that old man, holding meetings. And how … led by the Spirit.
text_no:
Å, for en følelse! Jeg visste ikke hva jeg skulle gjøre. Jeg la hånden på den eldre mannen og sa: "Gud, jeg forstår ikke dette. Din Ånd ledet meg hit, og den ledet ham hit. Vi kjenner ikke hverandre, men hvis Du har sendt ham, Herre, vil Du sikkert bekrefte Ditt Ord."
Jeg tok hånden av ham, og folk presset på rundt oss, og politiet prøvde å holde dem tilbake. Jeg trakk meg tilbake, og da sa han: "Takk, Herre. Takk, Herre."
Hans kone sa: "Ser du, kjære?"
Han svarte: "Selvfølgelig!"
Hun spurte: "Ser du virkelig, kjære?"
Han svarte: "Selvfølgelig!" Han sa: "Ser du den røde bilen der borte—den som skal ta forkynner Branham med?" Alle begynte å skrike. Der sto den.
For omtrent to-tre år siden, hørte jeg fra ham igjen. Han forkynner fortsatt Evangeliet og holder møter, ledet av Ånden.

text:
One day here in Fort Wayne, Indiana, when it was just coming the time of when this man was healed there, that sent me over to the King of England. And he was sitting there. Lehman was his name. And the meeting was going on, great signs. You Pentecostal people know how the Evangel packed that piece, and We The People, in there. That blind girl: when the whole United States examined it, the Medical Association took her up, see whether it was right or not. And that little girl by the name of Bethel had been blind. I picked her up in my arms, and begin to pray for her, and a vision struck. And said, "Thus saith the Lord," and there she was healed like that. And they even examined it everywhere.
It come in the Assembly of God paper, and it come out in We The People, that big book. We The People: wrote … ever … goes internationally, everywhere. That same meeting… Just a little bit after that, there was a man sitting there with multiple sclerosis in his back. And I was sitting there, and they'd been watching. Night … was the last night of the service. And they wanted that man to get where was I at. Some of you might have been in the Fort Wayne meeting. Old Dr. Bosworth and them was there. And they started taking this meeting. And they put this man in my path so that he'd just… And he wanted to touch the trouser legs of my … of my trouser legs going out.
And the poor fellow laying there with white shirt on. And the people walking over him. I said, "What a shame! Don't lay that man there. Pick him up." And then they started to put him back in a chair. They just even climbed up on top of the roof, and for, oh, I believe it was… I forget how many city blocks, twenty some odd city blocks, cars was parked everywhere, and people outside standing on the street. You was at the Fort Wayne meeting knows what it was.
text_no:
En dag her i Fort Wayne, Indiana, rett etter at denne mannen ble helbredet, sendte dette meg til Kongen av England. Lehman het han og satt der. Møtet pågikk, med store tegn. Dere pinsevenner vet hvordan Evangel pakkede lokalet, og We The People dekket det også. En blind jente: hele USA undersøkte saken, og Den medisinske foreningen tok det opp for å se om det var riktig. Den lille jenta ved navn Bethel hadde vært blind. Jeg løftet henne opp i armene mine og begynte å be for henne, og en visjon kom. Den sa, "Så sier Herren," og der ble hun helbredet. De undersøkte det overalt.
Det ble omtalt i Assemblies of God-avisen, og det sto i We The People, den store boken. We The People går internasjonalt. På samme møte... Litt senere satt en mann der med multippel sklerose i ryggen. Jeg satt der, og de hadde fulgt nøye med. Det var siste kvelden av møtene, og de ønsket at mannen skulle få komme nær meg. Noen av dere var kanskje på Fort Wayne-møtet. Gamle Dr. Bosworth og flere var der. De begynte å ta over møtet og plasserte denne mannen i min vei så han kunne røre ved buksekanten min.
Den stakkars mannen lå der med hvit skjorte, og folk gikk over ham. Jeg sa, "For en skam! Ikke la mannen ligge der. Løft ham opp." De begynte å sette ham tilbake i stolen. Folk klatret til og med opp på taket, og jeg tror det var over tjue kvartaler med biler parkert overalt, og folk stod utenfor på gaten. Dere som var på Fort Wayne-møtet vet hva det var.

text:
And that was right following Paul Rader there, my old friend, and so forth. Then, when I started, looked out, I seen a vision, of Mr. Lehman walking. And I know Mrs. Morgan, Mayo's nurse, give up with cancer years ago, laying on the dead list right now. And go to Louisville, Kentucky, and look and see if she hasn't died eight years ago. Come over to Jeffersonville Hospital and look at her nursing now. And so, she was supposed to've died eight years ago. Dead, done pronounced dead, and laying there yet, still on the dead list in Kentucky, of cancer research. And then, when she was there, she felt sorry for that man, because he looked so much like her husband. And I seen a vision of him walking.
But in the vision, He told me, "Go down and pray for that man." And I went down, put my hands upon him, right without a prayer card, or anything. Walked down and said, "The Lord said, 'Lay hands on you, brother, in the name of the Lord Jesus.'" And I walked back up. And I seen somebody laying on a cot. They was trying to move him over, and a man with arthritis touched me like that, and held on to my coat as I was coming by.
About four or five days after that, or maybe six days, I'll give you her address, if you want to write to them. This one was a farmer laying there with arthritis, and when the man pulled me, he turned around, and I seen him doing something on the farm. I said, "You're a farmer."
He said, "Yes, sir."
I said, "Don't worry, for 'Thus saith the Lord,' you're going to be well." And four days later, Mr. Lehman was going down the road, driving his car, and he looked over in the field, and here was this farmer sitting out there on a tractor, running the tractor, and he jumps out of his car, the man jumps off the tractor; they run grabbed at one another, lifted one another up and down praising God; how that the Lord had did it.
text_no:
Dette skjedde rett etter Paul Rader, min gamle venn, og så videre. Da jeg startet, så jeg opp og så et syn av Mr. Lehman som gikk. Jeg vet at Mrs. Morgan, Mayo-sykepleieren, som ble gitt opp med kreft for mange år siden, lå på dødslisten akkurat da. Gå til Louisville, Kentucky, og se om hun ikke døde for åtte år siden. Kom deretter til Jeffersonville sykehus og se henne nå, sykepleieren. Hun skulle ha dødd for åtte år siden, erklært død og fortsatt på dødslisten i Kentucky for kreftforskning. Da hun var der, syntes hun synd på den mannen, fordi han lignet så mye på hennes ektemann. Jeg så et syn av ham gående.
Men i synet fortalte Han meg, "Gå ned og be for den mannen." Jeg gikk ned, la hendene på ham uten bønnekort eller noe. Jeg sa, "Herren sa, 'Legg hendene på deg, bror, i Herrens Jesu navn.'" Så gikk jeg tilbake. Jeg så noen ligge på en båre. De forsøkte å flytte ham, og en mann med leddgikt rørte ved meg og holdt fast i kåpen min mens jeg gikk forbi.
Fire eller fem dager senere, eller kanskje seks, kan jeg gi deg adressen hennes hvis du vil skrive til dem. Dette var en bonde som lå der med leddgikt, og når mannen dro i meg, snudde jeg meg og så ham gjøre noe på gården. Jeg sa, "Du er en bonde."
Han sa, "Ja, sir."
Jeg sa, "Ikke bekymre deg, for 'Så sier Herren,' du kommer til å bli frisk." Fire dager senere kjørte Mr. Lehman ned veien, så ut i feltet og her satt bonden på en traktor og kjørte den. Mr. Lehman hoppet ut av bilen, mannen hoppet av traktoren; de løp mot hverandre og omfavnet hverandre, løftet hverandre opp og ned mens de priste Gud for hva Herren hadde gjort.

text:
The next morning there was so many people gathered in that Indiana Hotel, where I was staying, (That's the reason we keep the place a secret.) And the little bellhop come up, and he said, "Preacher, you'll never get out through that room this morning," said, "I'll tell you that." Said, "There's just too many standing down there."
Well, I wondered how I was going to get… Have wife and baby there; Mrs. Morgan was with us. And I said, "Well, I don't know. The women will have to have something to eat."
And so, after a while another little bellhop come up, said, "Preacher, I tell you what I can do. I can take you down in the basement if you don't mind climbing over some ashes, and it'll take you out in the alley, as you go out."
I said, "All right." So, he takes us down; we climbed over the ashes, and went out through the ash shoot in the basement. We was going down the street, and I had my coat up like this. I don't mean, don't want to be bad, but you just simply can't… You don't realize what a big meeting like that produces. And so, I was going down the street, was going to a little place called the Toddle's House, where we had been eating.
text_no:
Neste morgen var det samlet så mange mennesker på Indiana Hotel, der jeg bodde. (Det er grunnen til at vi holder stedet hemmelig.) En liten pikkolo kom opp og sa: "Forkynner, du vil aldri komme deg ut gjennom det rommet i morges," sa han. "Jeg sier deg det." Han sa: "Det står bare for mange der nede."
Jeg lurte på hvordan jeg skulle komme meg ut. Jeg hadde kone og baby der, og Mrs. Morgan var med oss. Jeg sa: "Vel, jeg vet ikke. Kvinnene må få noe å spise."
Etter en stund kom en annen pikkolo opp og sa: "Forkynner, jeg kan ta deg ned i kjelleren hvis du ikke har noe imot å klatre over noen askehøyer. Det vil føre deg ut i bakgaten."
Jeg sa: "Greit." Så han tok oss ned, vi klatret over asken og gikk ut gjennom askeskakten i kjelleren. På vei nedover gaten hadde jeg jakken over hodet. Jeg mener ikke å være frekk, men man skjønner ikke hva et så stort møte kan føre til. Vi var på vei til et lite sted kalt Toddle's House, hvor vi hadde spist.

text:
Morning before there, some great man out of this here Hudson Bay Company in Canada had been healed with a stomach trouble, sitting right in the place, when a vision come, told him who he was, and all about it, right there in the restaurant, told him he was healed. He said, "That's right, sir. I'm traveling through here," said, "that's exactly my name, and that's where I come from." He said, "I've been suffering with stomach trouble."
I said, "You're healed, sir."
And he said, "Well, bless your heart, boy." He come over there.
I said, "You just wait and see. You could go ahead and eat your breakfast, anything you want, 'cause the Lord has made you well."
I was going … I started to go down to that same place. The Holy Spirit said, "Move this way."
And I turned down that way. Mrs. Morgan said, "Where are you going?"
My wife said, "Look! It's the anointing of the Holy Spirit." Said, "Just follow along."
text_no:
Morgenen før var en innflytelsesrik mann fra Hudson Bay Company i Canada blitt helbredet for mageproblemer mens han satt i restauranten. En visjon hadde vist hvem han var og alt om ham, og fortalte ham at han var helbredet. Han sa: "Det stemmer, sir. Jeg er på gjennomreise," og fortsatte, "det er akkurat mitt navn, og der kommer jeg fra." Han la til: "Jeg har lidd av mageproblemer."
Jeg svarte: "Du er helbredet, sir."
Han sa: "Vel, Gud velsigne deg, gutt." Han kom bort til meg.
Jeg sa: "Vent og se. Du kan gå videre og spise frokosten din, hva du vil, for Herren har gjort deg frisk."
Jeg skulle til å dra ned til det samme stedet da Den Hellige Ånd sa: "Gå denne veien."
Jeg endret retning, og fru Morgan spurte: "Hvor skal du?"
Min kone svarte: "Se! Det er salvelsen av Den Hellige Ånd." Hun sa: "Bare følg med."

text:
So we went along, and I stopped in front of a place called Miller's Cafeteria. I looked up. I thought, Well, what am I doing here? And I looked and there was my wife, Mrs. Morgan was packing the baby. And I said, "Well…
She said, "Where are you going?"
I said, "Let's go in here."
So we went in, went down along the side, and I picked up, oh, some stuff we was going to eat, went and set it down. And just as I sat down like that, we bowed our head to pray, I heard somebody say, "Praise the Lord." And Mrs. Morgan looked over, and there was a woman and a man sitting right…
She said, "Uh-huh! Now you're caught, aren't you?"
And I said, "The Lord told me to come here."
text_no:
Vi gikk videre, og jeg stoppet foran et sted som het Miller's Kafeteria. Jeg så opp og tenkte, Hva gjør jeg her? Da så jeg min kone, fru Morgan, som bar babyen. Jeg sa: "Vel..."
Hun spurte: "Hvor skal du?"
Jeg svarte: "La oss gå inn her."
Vi gikk inn, tok oss langs siden, og jeg plukket opp noe mat vi skulle spise. Vi satte oss ned, og akkurat da vi bøyde hodene for å be, hørte jeg noen si, "Pris Herren." Fru Morgan så bort, og der satt en kvinne og en mann.
Hun sa: "Aha! Nå er du tatt, ikke sant?"
Jeg svarte: "Herren ba meg komme hit."

text:
This lady walked over, she said, "Brother Branham, listen." I looked over at the table there wasn't nothing laying on the table. And she said, "I've been sitting here ever since this opened up this morning." She said, "Here's my brother." Said, "The heart is enlarged so big, till it's come through the diaphragm someway." She said, "The doctor said that he can't live."
Said, "We followed you meeting after meeting till we've spent all the money we had." And she said, "I sold my cow, we live in Texas," said, "and I sold my cow for enough money to bring brother here." Said, "We're absolutely, totally broke." And said, "I was in such a desperate fix, I prayed last night," said, "all night long. And this morning," said, "I fell asleep, and I had a dream. And the Lord told me to come and find Miller's Cafeteria, and be here by nine o'clock." And looked up, and it was just exactly nine o'clock. You know what had happened, don't you?
text_no:
Denne kvinnen kom bort og sa, "Bror Branham, hør." Jeg så på bordet; det lå ingenting der. Hun sa, "Jeg har sittet her siden det åpnet i morges." Hun fortsatte, "Her er min bror. Hjertet er så stort at det har trengt gjennom mellomgulvet på et vis." Hun sa, "Legen har sagt at han ikke kan overleve."
Hun sa, "Vi har fulgt deg fra møte til møte og brukt opp alle pengene vi hadde." Hun sa, "Jeg solgte kua mi. Vi bor i Texas, og jeg solgte kua mi for å få nok penger til å få bror hit." Hun sa, "Vi er fullstendig blakke." Hun fortsatte, "Jeg var i en så desperat situasjon at jeg ba hele natten i går. Og denne morgenen sovnet jeg og hadde en drøm. Herren ba meg komme til Miller's Cafeteria og være her innen klokken ni." Da hun så på klokken, var den akkurat ni. Du skjønner hva som hadde skjedd, ikke sant?

text:
After her brother was healed, he started crying, like that. And I turned around, didn't want my breakfast, started walking out. Marjorie and (that's the nurse), and my wife was going out. And I said, "Something is telling me, 'Go outside.'" And just as I went outside, a little woman dressed in black, she just fell on the street. Her husband runs this big spaghetti company in Chicago. She's in pretty near every meeting: (May be here for all I know, if she knowed I's here, would be here.) She and her husband's a multimillionaire.
She had come from Mayo Brothers, and she was, oh, swelled way out with a malignant cancer, a growth that could not be cured. Mayo's wouldn't even touch it. She said, "Brother Branham," she said, "I followed meeting after meeting;" but she said, "I've got in such a place, till I can't go any farther." And she said, "The strangest thing happened." Said, "This morning, I dreamed a dream, that I should be standing here in front of this cafeteria at ten minutes after nine." There it was. That just happens constantly.
text_no:
Etter at hennes bror ble helbredet, begynte han å gråte. Jeg snudde meg rundt, mistet lysten på frokosten og begynte å gå ut. Marjorie (sykepleieren) og min kone var på vei ut. Jeg sa: "Noe sier meg, 'Gå ut.'" Da jeg gikk utenfor, falt en liten kvinne kledd i svart om på gaten. Mannen hennes driver et stort spaghettifirma i Chicago. Hun er nesten på hvert møte, og hun kunne godt være her i dag dersom hun visste at jeg var her. Hun og mannen hennes er multimillionærer.
Hun hadde vært hos Mayo-brødrene og hadde en stor, ondartet svulst som ikke kunne kureres. Mayo-klinikken ville ikke en gang røre den. Hun sa: "Bror Branham, jeg har fulgt møtene gang på gang, men jeg har kommet til et punkt hvor jeg ikke kan gå lenger." Hun sa videre: "Det merkeligste skjedde. I morges drømte jeg at jeg skulle stå foran denne kafeteriaen klokken ti over ni." Og der var hun. Slike ting skjer stadig.

text:
I went on up to the corner. And my wife said … I said, "You go on over to the drug store and get some little books there for the little kid." Said, I said, "You all go on in just on up through," and I said, "I'll be in after while." I said, "He's leading me, I don't know where to go now, but I feel that you all should go … go on."
So they started on. And I stood there. There was some fishing tackle and things, up there. And I thought I'd go up here and act like I was looking at fishing tackle. And I was standing there, didn't see nobody. And I said, "Lord Jesus, what do You want me to do, Father." I said, "What is it You want me to do?" Kept praying like that.
In a few minutes, He said, "Go to the corner." I walked out, walked up to the corner (You may call this fiction; it's on record, and it's on record in Heaven, too.) And I stood there, and those big Irish cops. I went across the other corner. Though the big Irish cop out there, blow a whistle and the pedestrians would cross and so forth. Kept on, I stood there a little while. After a while another group come across. And way in the back of them was a little woman with one of these little black-checkered dresses on, a little tam. She had a pocketbook on her arm. She was walking across like this.
text_no:
Jeg gikk opp til hjørnet. Min kone sa jeg kunne gå til apoteket og kjøpe noen små bøker til barnet. Jeg sa, "Dere kan gå videre, jeg kommer etter hvert." Jeg fortsatte, "Han leder meg, og jeg vet ikke hvor jeg skal nå, men dere bør bare fortsette."
Så de gikk videre, og jeg ble stående der. Det var noe fiskeredskap der, og jeg tenkte at jeg kunne late som om jeg så på dem. Jeg sto der og så ingen. Så ba jeg, "Herre Jesus, hva vil Du at jeg skal gjøre, Far?" Jeg fortsatte å be slik.
Etter noen minutter hørte jeg, "Gå til hjørnet." Jeg gikk ut og opp til hjørnet. (Dette kan virke som fiksjon, men det er registrert både her og i Himmelen.) Jeg sto der, og de store irske politimennene dirigerte trafikken. 
Jeg gikk til det andre hjørnet. En av de store irske politimennene blåste i fløyten, og fotgjengerne krysset gaten. Jeg ble stående en stund. Etter en stund kom en ny gruppe fotgjengere. Lengst bak i gruppen var det en liten kvinne med en rutete svart kjole og en liten hatt. Hun hadde en veske på armen og gikk over gata.

text:
The Holy Spirit said, "Come up close to her." I walked up pretty close to her, and she passed like that, went on by, never said a word.
Well, I thought, isn't that strange? I never knew that.
And she was studying. She had her head down. She just turned, she said, "Oh, Brother Branham." She looked back like that.
And I said, "What's the matter, sister?"
She said, "Oh, am I dreaming?" She said, "Is it really you, Brother Branham?"
I said, "Yes, ma'am. What's the matter, sister?"
"Oh!" She said. Said, "Brother Branham, I'm from Canada, we're only allowed a hundred and fifty dollars a year of American money." Said, "I've spent every penny I had." Said, "I slept in a hotel lobby for two nights, and this morning I was going over here to hitchhike to go back to Canada." She said, on the road… A young woman, not over about twenty-five years old, or thirty. Said, "I was going back to Canada, hitchhike back." Said, "I had five cents for a cup of coffee, and that's the last penny I had. And I was going out that way, to hit that highway, and Something said, 'Turn around this way, and start walking.'"
Oh, my! Those that are children of God are led by the Spirit of God. And she was crossed… I said, "What's your trouble, sister?"
She said, "My arm, brother." And she said, "Look at here!" And her hand come out like that, just as normal and straight as it could be. Perfectly normal and well.
Oh, He's God, tonight. The same One that could lead Simeon, could do the same thing.
text_no:
Den Hellige Ånd sa: "Gå nærmere henne." Jeg gikk ganske nær henne, og hun gikk forbi uten å si et ord.
Vel, jeg tenkte, er ikke det merkelig? Jeg visste ikke det.
Hun studerte. Hun hadde hodet bøyd. Hun snudde seg bare, og sa: "Å, Bror Branham." Hun så tilbake slik.
Og jeg sa: "Hva er i veien, søster?"
Hun sa: "Å, drømmer jeg? Er det virkelig deg, Bror Branham?"
Jeg sa: "Ja, frue. Hva er i veien, søster?"
"Å!" Hun sa, "Bror Branham, jeg er fra Canada. Vi har bare lov til å ha hundreogfemti dollar i amerikanske penger per år." Hun sa: "Jeg har brukt hver eneste krone jeg hadde." Hun sa: "Jeg sov i en hotellobby i to netter, og i morges skulle jeg gå hit for å haike tilbake til Canada." Hun sa at på veien... En ung kvinne, ikke mer enn tjuefem eller tretti år gammel. Hun sa: "Jeg skulle haike tilbake til Canada." Hun sa: "Jeg hadde fem cent for en kopp kaffe, og det var den siste krona jeg hadde. Og jeg var på vei dit for å treffe veien, og noe sa: 'Snudd hit og begynn å gå.'"
Å, min! De som er Guds barn leds av Guds Ånd. Og hun ble krysset... Jeg sa: "Hva er problemet ditt, søster?"
Hun sa: "Min arm, Bror." Og hun sa: "Se her!" Hånden hennes kom fram slik, helt normal og rett. Perfekt normal og frisk.
Å, Han er Gud, i kveld. Den samme som kunne lede Simeon, kan gjøre det samme.

text:
Just once, a little… I was crossing coming from Dallas, Texas, from a convention, just recently. About last … about three years ago. I was crossing over and a storm struck us in a plane, and we were grounded. And they put us in a big hotel there. I couldn't afford to stay in the hotel, like that, but the air service put us … Peabody Hotel, in Memphis, Tennessee. And that night, we was up there. They called us the next morning, said, "the plane will be ready to leave at seven o'clock." I'd wrote some letters, anointed some handkerchiefs and so forth, and was going to put it in the mail. So I got up, got the mail and started down the street, was going down to put it in the mailbox, and I was going out down there. Walking along. And the Holy Spirit said, "Stop!"
Well, I thought, That was just… I just imagined I heard that. Went a little farther. And Something begin to go, "Whhewww." I stopped and I said, "Father, what is it that You would have me do?" I stepped back up to a place like this; it was just a little piece from the post office." And I said, "What would You have me do, Father?" And I stood there, about ten minutes. I said, "My, it's getting late. What would You have me do?" I just stood there a little bit.
Directly, I heard Something say, "Turn and go the other way." Well, I walked out and turned.
text_no:
For omtrent tre år siden, krysset jeg fra Dallas, Texas etter et stevne. Under flyturen ble vi truffet av en storm og måtte nødlande. Vi ble plassert på et stort hotell, Peabody Hotel, i Memphis, Tennessee. Jeg hadde ikke råd til å bo der, men flyselskapet ordnet opp.
Neste morgen fikk vi beskjed om at flyet skulle gå klokken syv. Jeg hadde skrevet noen brev og salvet noen lommetørklær, og skulle poste dem. Jeg gikk ut på gaten for å finne en postkasse. Mens jeg gikk, sa Den Hellige Ånd plutselig, "Stopp!"
Først trodde jeg at jeg innbilte meg det, men noen skritt videre hørte jeg igjen, "Whhewww." Jeg stanset og spurte, "Far, hva vil Du at jeg skal gjøre?" Jeg sto ved et sted like ved postkontoret og spurte igjen, "Far, hva vil Du at jeg skal gjøre?" Etter omtrent ti minutter, ble jeg utålmodig og spurte igjen, "Hva vil Du at jeg skal gjøre?" Jeg sto stille litt til.
Til slutt hørte jeg, "Snu og gå den andre veien." Jeg snudde og gikk videre.

text:
I thought, Maybe there's somebody coming along there, maybe some trouble, maybe bypassing from an accident. I don't know. I went on, just kept walking, and walking, and walking, passed on the hotel, went on down, on down, on down, down towards the river, way down towards the North Memphis there, on down through that way.
I got down amongst the little colored haunts like down there, little houses and things, way down there. And it was coming up just about… The sun was way up, way past time for me to catch the plane. I thought, Oh, my.
But the Holy Spirit still urging me on, "Go on, move on."
I was walking on down the street, I was singing that little song you Pentecostal people sing about: "There's people almost everywhere, whose hearts are all on flame, With fire that fell on Pentecost, And cleansed and made them clean." You know it, I guess. I was trying just to learn it. I said,
There are people almost everywhere,
Whose hearts are all on flame
The fire that fell on Pentecost,
Which cleansed and made them clean;
Oh, it's burning now within my heart,
Oh, glory to His name!
I'm so glad that I can say I'm one of them.
text_no:
Jeg tenkte: Kanskje det er noen som kommer forbi, kanskje det har oppstått et problem, kanskje noen unngår en ulykke. Jeg vet ikke. Jeg fortsatte å gå og gå, gikk forbi hotellet, fortsatte nedover mot elven, langt ned mot Nord-Memphis, gjennom det området.
Jeg kom ned blant de små husene der nede, små fargerike hjem og lignende, langt der nede. Solen sto høyt på himmelen, og det var lenge forbi tiden for å rekke flyet. Jeg tenkte, Å, nei.
Men Den Hellige Ånd fortsatte å oppmuntre meg, "Gå videre, fortsett."
Jeg gikk nedover gaten mens jeg sang den lille sangen dere pinsevenner synger om: "Det er folk nesten overalt, hvis hjerter brenner med ild som falt på pinse, og renset og gjorde dem rene." Du kjenner den nok. Jeg prøvde bare å lære den.
Jeg sa:
Det er folk nesten overalt,
Hvis hjerter brenner
Med ilden som falt på pinse,
Som renset og gjorde dem rene;
Å, den brenner nå i mitt hjerte,
Ære til Hans navn!
Jeg er så glad for at jeg kan si jeg er en av dem.

text:
I was going along, singing like that. I happened to look down there on the street, and there around a little, old, white-washed fence, an old colored woman, typical Aunt Jemima, about a hundred yards from me; had a man's shirt tied around her head like this, and she was leaning over a little old gate, like that. And she was looking down the street. I just kind of quit singing, started walking on. I got up there, grinning like that, and the big tears running off them big fat cheeks, like that. She just kind of grinned at me.
She said, "Good morning, Parson."
I said, "Good morning, Auntie."
Started walking on like that, and Something said to me… I said, "Say, what'd you call me?"
She said, "Parson!"
I said, "How did you know I was a Parson?"
She said, "Did you ever read that story in the Bible about the Shunammite woman?"
I said, "Yes, ma'am."
text_no:
Jeg gikk langs og sang. Jeg kikket ned på gaten og så en eldre, farget kvinne rundt en liten, hvitmalt gjerde. Hun var typisk en "Aunt Jemima"-type, omtrent hundre meter fra meg. Hun hadde en manns skjorte knyttet rundt hodet og lente seg over en liten gammel port, og så nedover gaten. Jeg stoppet å synge og begynte å gå videre. Da jeg kom nærmere, så jeg at de store tårene trillet nedover de fyldige kinnene hennes, og hun smilte litt til meg.
Hun sa, "God morgen, Pastor."
Jeg svarte, "God morgen, tante."
Jeg fortsatte å gå, men noe fikk meg til å snu meg og si: "Unnskyld, hva kalte du meg?"
Hun sa, "Pastor!"
Jeg spurte, "Hvordan visste du at jeg var en Pastor?"
Hun svarte, "Har du noen gang lest historien i Bibelen om kvinnen fra Sjunem?"
Jeg sa, "Ja, frue."

text:
She said, "I's that kind of a woman." She said, "I couldn't have no children. And I promised the Lord, if He would give me a child, that I would raise it for His glory." Said, "I's a wash woman." And she said, "And I's a Christian." And she said, "And the Lord give me a fine boy. And I raised him up."
Said, "But, I'm sorry he took the wrong road." And said, "He took a horrible disease---syphilitic." And said, "And he got out amongst the wrong company," and said, "and he went so long, and getting sick," said, "I didn't think of anything like that till it's done eat him, till he's full of puss in his blood, and the holes has eat through his heart until his blood drops back when his heart beats it." And says, "He's going to die." And said, "He's laying in there," and said, "he has been unconscious for two days." And said, "I just couldn't stand it to see my baby die like that, unsaved."
text_no:
Hun sa: "Jeg er den typen kvinne." Hun sa: "Jeg kunne ikke få barn. Og jeg lovet Herren at hvis Han ga meg et barn, ville jeg oppdra det for Hans ære." Hun sa: "Jeg er en vaskekone. Og jeg er kristen." Hun sa: "Og Herren ga meg en flott gutt, som jeg oppdro."
Hun sa: "Men jeg er lei meg for at han tok feil vei." Hun sa: "Han pådro seg en forferdelig sykdom – syfilis." Hun sa: "Han kom ut blant feil selskap, og han ble syk. Jeg tenkte ikke på noe slikt før det hadde spist ham opp. Blodet hans er fullt av puss, og hull har spist gjennom hjertet hans slik at blodet renner tilbake når hjertet slår." Hun sa: "Han kommer til å dø. Han har ligget der inne bevisstløs i to dager, og jeg orker ikke se min baby dø slik, uselv frelst."

text:
And she said, "I prayed all night, Lord." Said, "Lord, I's Your servant. And You give me this baby, but where's Your Elisha?" Said, "What must I do?" And said, "This morning, about four o'clock," said, "the Lord told me in a dream to go and stand at this gate," and said, "you'd be coming, wearing a brown suit, and a brown hat." She said, "The only thing that I missed, is where is that little briefcase that you was supposed to have?" And I had left it in the hotel.
Don't you believe God still is the same God that was with Simeon?
I said, "My name is Branham, Auntie."
She said, "Branham?"
I said, "Did you ever hear of me?"
She said, "No, sir, I don't believe I ever did."
And I said, "I pray for the sick."
She said, "You do?"
And I said, "Yes."
She said, "Would you come in?"
text_no:
Hun sa: "Jeg ba hele natten, Herre." Hun sa, "Herre, Jeg er Din tjener. Du ga meg dette barnet, men hvor er Din Elisha?" Hun spurte, "Hva skal jeg gjøre?" Og hun fortsatte, "I morges, rundt klokken fire, fortalte Herren meg i en drøm å gå og stå ved denne porten," og sa, "du ville komme, iført en brun dress og en brun hatt." Hun sa, "Det eneste jeg mangler er den lille stresskofferten som du skulle ha hatt med." Jeg hadde etterlatt den på hotellet.
Tror dere ikke at Gud fortsatt er den samme Gud som var med Simeon?
Jeg sa, "Mitt navn er Branham, tante."
Hun spurte, "Branham?"
Jeg sa, "Har du noensinne hørt om meg?"
Hun svarte, "Nei, sir, jeg tror ikke det."
Jeg sa, "Jeg ber for de syke."
Hun sa, "Gjør du det?"
Jeg svarte, "Ja."
Hun ba meg inn: "Vil du komme inn?"

text:
And she opened up the gate, and it had a chain with an old plow point hanging on it. I don't know whether you know what a plow point is or not, it keeps… And I walked in that little gate. And she opened the door, that little old two-room, white-washed cabin down there by the river bank. And I walked in there. Listen.
There was a big sign on the door: "God bless our home," when you come in. No rug on the floor, a little old poster bed, if you know what it is there, a little iron bedstead. And a big healthy-looking boy, laying there, looked about would weigh a hundred and eighty pounds, about eighteen, twenty years old. And just carrying on awful, unconscious. No floor on the rug … no rug on the floor, rather, and holes that big in the floor, big cracks like that.
text_no:
Hun åpnet porten, som var lenket med et gammelt plogjern. Jeg vet ikke om du vet hva et plogjern er, men det holder ... Jeg gikk gjennom den lille porten. Hun åpnet døren til den gamle, hvitkalkede hytta med to rom nede ved elvebredden. Jeg gikk inn der. Lytt.
Det var et stort skilt på døren: "Gud velsigne vårt hjem," ved inngangen. Ingen teppe på gulvet, bare en gammel køyeseng, hvis du vet hva det er, med en liten jernseng ved siden av. En stor, sunn gutt lå der, så ut til å veie rundt åtti kilo, omtrent atten til tjue år gammel. Han lå der bevisstløs og oppførte seg urolig. Gulvet hadde store sprekker; det var ingen teppe på gulvet.

text:
I've walked into king's palaces. I've been in some of the most lovely homes there is in the world, but I never was anymore … felt anymore welcomed than I did in that little colored haunt that morning. I walked in there knowing that the God of Heaven was with her. I didn't know what to do.
And he was going, "Mmm, mmm." He said, "It's so dark. It's so dark." Had the quilt in his hand like this, going "Mmm, mmm."
And she'd pat him. She said, "Mammy's baby."
I thought, Yes, in disgrace, nearly twenty years old, I suppose. And no matter how disgraceful he was, how much trouble he had got into, he's still "mammy's baby." That's a mother's love. And if the love of a mother will do that, what will the love of God do? She patted him, kissed him on his forehead.
I said, "What's he talking about?"
She said, "Parson, he says…" Said, "He don't know what he's talking about." Said, "He's been unconscious. The doctor says he ain't never going to wake up." And said, "Parson, I just can't see him go like that." Said, "He keeps saying he's lost out in a big ocean somewhere. And he's in a little boat, and it's all dark around him." Said, "That just breaks my heart." She said, "You pray that God will save my baby, before he die, won't you?"
text_no:
Jeg har gått inn i kongens palasser. Jeg har vært i noen av de vakreste hjemmene i verden, men jeg har aldri følt meg mer velkommen enn jeg gjorde i den lille fargede boligen den morgenen. Jeg gikk inn der og visste at Himmelens Gud var med henne. Jeg visste ikke hva jeg skulle gjøre.
Han sa, "Mmm, mmm." Han sa, "Det er så mørkt. Det er så mørkt," mens han holdt teppet i hendene og sa, "Mmm, mmm."
Hun klappet ham og sa, "Mammys baby."
Jeg tenkte: Ja, i vanære, nesten tjue år gammel, antar jeg. Uansett hvor vanærende han var, og hvor mye trøbbel han hadde kommet i, var han fortsatt "mammys baby." Det er en mors kjærlighet. Og hvis en mors kjærlighet kan gjøre det, hva kan ikke Guds kjærlighet gjøre? Hun klappet ham og kysset ham på pannen.
Jeg spurte, "Hva snakker han om?"
Hun svarte, "Pastor, han sier…" Hun sa, "Han vet ikke hva han snakker om." Hun sa, "Han har vært bevisstløs. Legen sier at han aldri vil våkne opp igjen." Hun sa, "Pastor, jeg klarer ikke å se ham gå bort slik." Hun fortsatte, "Han sier stadig at han er tapt i et stort hav et sted. Han er i en liten båt, og det er helt mørkt rundt ham." Hun sa, "Det bare knuser hjertet mitt." Hun sa, "Du vil be om at Gud vil redde babyen min før han dør, ikke sant?"

text:
I said, "Yes, ma'am, Auntie." I said, "Shall we bow?" And I knelt at the foot of the bed. I took hold of his feet, just as cold as they could be, just like death. And she said … she knelt down. I said, "You lead us in prayer, Auntie." And you talk about praying. "Mmm." She got down there just as quiet.
She said, "Now, dear Lord," she said, "I stood out there," and her back was wet from the dew of that morning. She had been standing there all that time. God told her, and she was standing there waiting. I didn't know what to do.
So she said, "Dear Lord," she said, "don't let my darling baby die in disgrace like that." Said, "I want to see him in Heaven, where he'll never get in anymore troubles." Said, "Won't You please, dear Lord, let me hear him say with his own lips that he is saved again." And like that. A prayer… And I couldn't keep tears from running down my cheeks. I looked at that poor old mammy and I looked down there.
I thought, O God!
And when she got through praying like that, she said, "Will you pray now, Parson, to God save him?"
text_no:
Jeg sa: "Ja, tante." Jeg sa: "Skal vi bøye oss?" Så knelte jeg ved fotenden av sengen. Jeg tok tak i føttene hans, som var iskald og dødslignende. Hun knelte også. Jeg sa: "Du leder oss i bønn, tante." Og du snakker om å be. "Mmm." Hun knelte der helt stille.
Hun sa: "Nå, kjære Herre," hun sa, "jeg sto der ute," og ryggen hennes var våt av morgenduggen. Hun hadde stått der hele tiden. Gud hadde talt til henne, og hun ventet. Jeg visste ikke hva jeg skulle gjøre.
Hun fortsatte: "Kjære Herre," hun sa, "ikke la min kjære baby dø i vanære." Hun sa: "Jeg vil se ham i Himmelen, der han aldri vil komme i problemer igjen." Hun ba: "Vær så snill, kjære Herre, la meg høre ham si med egne lepper at han er frelst igjen." En slik bønn... Jeg kunne ikke holde tårene tilbake. Jeg så på den stakkars gamle kvinnemoren og ned på ham.
Jeg tenkte, å Gud!
Da hun var ferdig med å be slik, sa hun: "Vil du be nå, Pastor, om at Gud skal frelse ham?"

text:
I walked over there, laid my hands on his black feet. I said, "Heavenly Father, You in Your sovereign grace, I don't know why I'm here." I said, "My plane has been gone for two hours." I said, "Why did You bring me down here in this place and I don't know what to do? Looks like that this is where You've sent me. But You led me here, I didn't know no more than to come. And to think that You would bring me down here to this place! What would You have me to do, dear God? I pray for mercy. Whatever it is You want me to do, I don't know, Lord. What is it You wish?"
I heard him going, "Mmm, mmm." Said, "Mammy!"
She said, "What's mammy's baby want?"
Said, "Oh, mammy." Said, "It's getting light in this room." And in five minutes from then, he was sitting on the side of the bed, with his arm around his mammy, the other arm around me, praising God.
text_no:
Jeg gikk bort og la hendene mine på føttene hans. Jeg sa, "Himmelske Far, i Din suverene nåde, jeg vet ikke hvorfor jeg er her." Jeg fortsatte, "Flyet mitt har allerede gått for to timer siden. Hvorfor har Du ført meg hit til dette stedet når jeg ikke vet hva jeg skal gjøre? Det ser ut som om det er her Du har sendt meg. Men Du ledet meg hit, og jeg visste ikke noe annet enn å komme. Og å tenke at Du ville bringe meg hit! Hva ønsker Du at jeg skal gjøre, kjære Gud? Jeg ber om nåde. Uansett hva det er Du ønsker at jeg skal gjøre, jeg vet ikke, Herre. Hva er det Du ønsker?"
Jeg hørte ham si, "Mmm, mmm." Han sa, "Mammy!"
Hun svarte, "Hva ønsker mammy's baby?"
Han sa, "Åh, mammy." Han fortsatte, "Det blir lyst i rommet." Fem minutter senere satt han på sengekanten med armen rundt moren sin og den andre armen rundt meg, og priset Gud.

text:
About two years later, I pulled in there on a train, I got off to get me a sandwich there, and I was going on to California. And I got off a train there, and started running down, along the place there towards… I heard somebody holler, "Hello, Parson Branham!"
I looked around, there was a redcap standing there, big smile on his face. I said, "How do you do, sir."
Said, "Don't you know me?"
And I said, "No, I don't!"
He said, "You remember one morning, the Holy Spirit led you down to my mammy's house where I was dying?"
I said, "Are you that boy?"
Said, "Yes." He said, "I's not only healed, but," said, "I's saved now, Parson Branham." Hallelujah! Praise God!
Your kind Heavenly Father that would ground… And listen, when I got back to the hotel, I called them to tell them that I'd be late, and I was just exactly on time. God kept that plane on the ground, and I caught it anyhow and went home.
text_no:
Omtrent to år senere ankom jeg samme sted med tog. Jeg gikk av for å kjøpe en sandwich før jeg skulle videre til California. Da jeg steg av toget og begynte å gå langs plattformen, hørte jeg noen rope: "Hallo, Pastor Branham!"
Jeg så meg rundt og fikk øye på en portør med et stort smil. Jeg sa: "God dag, sir."
Han spurte: "Kjenner du meg ikke igjen?"
Jeg svarte: "Nei, det gjør jeg ikke."
Han sa: "Husker du en morgen da Den Hellige Ånd ledet Deg til mammas hus hvor jeg lå og var døende?"
Jeg spurte: "Er det deg?"
Han svarte: "Ja." Han la til: "Jeg er ikke bare helbredet, men jeg er også blitt frelst nå, Pastor Branham." Halleluja! Pris Gud!
Vår kjære Himmelske Far... Og hør, da jeg kom tilbake til hotellet, ringte jeg og sa fra at jeg ville bli forsinket, men jeg kom akkurat til avtalt tid. Gud holdt igjen flyet, og jeg rakk det likevel og kom hjem.

text:
I tell you, "Amazing grace how sweet the sound that would save a wretch like me. Once was lost, but now I'm found. I was blind but now I see." Hallelujah!
To think of the sovereign grace of God that would ground that airplane up there, in the air, ground that plane, for the sake of an ignorant colored woman. Hallelujah! Oh, my Lord!
He's interested in anything that we do for His glory. He's God. He loves us. His amazing grace. His sweet… Oh, how sweet the sound that saved a poor wretch like me. Yes!
Oh, Christians, oh, it makes my heart jump for joy. My, mind, how those experiences sweep through my mind. Sure. I'm expecting Him. I believe that He's going to do the exceedingly abundantly. I know that He's here tonight beyond any shadow of doubt, the same Holy Spirit that led there, that same Angel of God… I don't have to wait on Him, He's right here now. He's right here anointing me, right now. The whole crowd is becoming milky looking over there. That same Angel of the Lord. He's here. His love and grace is the same tonight. His attitude towards any person in here is the same as it was towards that poor ignorant colored woman. Yes, sir. And many thousands of the others that I could quote.
text_no:
Jeg sier deg, "For en vidunderlig nåde, hvor søt lyden som frelste en elendig sjel som meg. Var en gang fortapt, men nå er jeg funnet. Jeg var blind, men nå ser jeg." Halleluja!
Tenk på Guds suverene nåde som kunne få et fly til å lande der oppe i luften, få det flyet til å lande, for en uvitende farget kvinner skyld. Halleluja! Å, min Herre!
Han er interessert i alt vi gjør for Hans ære. Han er Gud. Han elsker oss. Hans fantastiske nåde. Hans søte… Å, hvor søt lyden som frelste en fattig elendig som meg. Ja!
Å, kristne, å, det får hjertet mitt til å hoppe av glede. Min Gud, hvordan disse erfaringene sveiper gjennom mitt sinn. Selvfølgelig. Jeg forventer Ham. Jeg tror at Han kommer til å gjøre det overveldende rikelig. Jeg vet at Han er her i kveld uten skygge av tvil, den samme Hellige Ånd som ledet der, den samme Herrens Engel… Jeg trenger ikke vente på Ham, Han er rett her nå. Han salver meg akkurat nå. Hele menigheten blir melkaktig å se på der borte. Den samme Herrens Engel. Han er her. Hans kjærlighet og nåde er den samme i kveld. Hans holdning til enhver person her inne er den samme som den var til den fattige, uvitende fargede kvinnen. Ja, sir. Og mange tusen andre som jeg kunne sitere.

text:
What will it be when I cross over the ties yonder some of these days and strike the other side? And see those dear people face-to-face, when I can sit with them through ceaseless ages, talking of the love of God. No wonder His children will crown Him King of kings and Lord of lords. He's raised from the dead. He's a living Christ tonight. He's right here in this building now. His power is predominate. His power can do anything that you'll let it do, every one of you here. We're sitting in heavenly places in Christ Jesus. Do you believe it? We are.
The only thing I'm asking you to do is be expecting that you're going to get healed. Believing you're going to get… Do you believe it? Are you expecting to go out of here tonight, well? Certainly!
text_no:
Hvordan vil det være når jeg en dag krysser over til den andre siden og ser disse kjære menneskene ansikt til ansikt? Når jeg kan sitte med dem gjennom tidens evighet, og snakke om Guds kjærlighet. Ikke rart Hans barn vil krone Ham som Kongenes Konge og Herrenes Herre. Han er oppstått fra de døde. Han er en levende Kristus i kveld. Han er her nå, i denne bygningen. Hans kraft er overveldende. Hans kraft kan gjøre alt du lar Den gjøre, for hver og én av dere her. Vi sitter i himmelske steder i Kristus Jesus. Tror dere det? Det gjør vi.
Det eneste jeg ber deg om er å forvente at du skal bli helbredet. Tror du det? Er du forventningsfull om å gå ut herfra i kveld, frisk? Absolutt!

text:
You can call a prayer line now, bring the people up. Them people that comes up on this place here, no sign they're going to get healed. There's no sign at all. You don't even have to come up here. The only thing you have to do is to have faith and believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God that raised from the dead. And He's alive here, among us. He's just as much out there as He is up here. The only thing that's lacking is your faith in Him.
Everything that God could do is already done; it's your time to act next. He sent His Son to take your sins. He sent His Son to take your worry. He sent His Son to take your diseases. He sent His Son to heal your afflictions. And Jesus paid the price and put it in Calvary, and the only thing you have to do is look to it. The preacher comes with the Word and preaches it, lays it down, and shows you it's God's will. And if you won't take God's Word, to me, that would be enough. But I'm not God. God is in His mercy. Besides that, He sends on in the church prophets, and teachers, and evangelists, and everything to get to magnify and get you to the point He's not willing than any should perish, but that all might come to repentance. His blessings is for each one of you. Hallelujah!
The Holy Spirit already moving over that audience right now. Here, I'll tell you something. I won't call the prayer line. I challenge every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ to look this way and to believe that the Gospel truth is the truth.
text_no:
Du kan nå kalle opp bønnekøen og føre folk frem. De som kommer opp på denne plassen, har ingen garanti for å bli helbredet. Det finnes ingen tegn på det. Du trenger ikke engang komme opp her. Det eneste du må gjøre, er å ha tro og tro at Jesus Kristus er Guds Sønn som sto opp fra de døde. Og Han er levende her, blant oss. Han er like mye der ute som Han er her oppe. Det eneste som mangler, er din tro på Ham.
Alt som Gud kunne gjøre, er allerede gjort; nå er det din tur til å handle. Han sendte Sin Sønn for å ta bort dine synder. Han sendte Sin Sønn for å fjerne dine bekymringer. Han sendte Sin Sønn for å ta bort dine sykdommer. Han sendte Sin Sønn for å helbrede dine lidelser. Jesus betalte prisen og plasserte den på Golgata, og det eneste du trenger å gjøre, er å se til Ham. Forkynneren kommer med Ordet og forkynner det, fremlegger det og viser deg at det er Guds vilje.
Dersom du ikke vil ta Guds Ord, ville det for meg være nok. Men jeg er ikke Gud. Gud i Sin nåde sender også profeter, lærere og evangelister inn i menigheten, alt for å fremheve og få deg til å forstå at Han ikke ønsker at noen skal gå fortapt, men at alle skal komme til omvendelse. Hans velsignelser er for hver og en av dere. Halleluja!
Den Hellige Ånd beveger seg allerede over forsamlingen akkurat nå. Jeg skal fortelle dere noe. Jeg vil ikke kalle opp bønnekøen. Jeg utfordrer hver og en av dere i Jesu Kristi navn til å se denne vei og tro at Evangeliets sannhet er sannheten.

text:
How many in here that don't have prayer cards wants to be prayed for? I want somebody that ain't got prayer card. Raise your hand. All right. You got a prayer card, lady? You want to be prayed for, sitting right there?
Stand up to your feet just a minute. I don't know you. You're a perfect stranger to me. God does know you. Isn't that right? Do you believe me to be His servant? With all your heart? If our Lord Jesus is raised from the dead, I testify the truth. And if the truth is… The truth is the truth. God's obligated to give the truth. You're suffering with high blood pressure. Isn't that right? If that's right, raise up your hand.
Here's another thing. You're not from here. You come from New York City, somewhere up in that country there. You come this way. You've been in the meeting before. You were in a meeting, and that was… I see Mrs. Brown, an old woman, standing. It was in a New York meeting. It was my meeting and you were healed. And you had cancer, didn't you? And you're healed of high blood pressure now. Go on your road rejoicing. Jesus Christ makes you whole.
text_no:
Hvor mange her inne som ikke har bønnekort ønsker å bli bedt for? Jeg vil ha noen som ikke har bønnekort. Løft hånden. OK. Har du et bønnekort, frue? Du vil bli bedt for, sittende der?
Reis deg opp et øyeblikk. Jeg kjenner deg ikke. Du er en fullstendig fremmed for meg. Gud kjenner deg. Er ikke det riktig? Tror du at jeg er Hans tjener? Av hele ditt hjerte? Hvis vår Herre Jesus er oppstått fra de døde, vitner jeg om sannheten. Og hvis sannheten er... Sannheten er sannheten. Gud er forpliktet til å gi sannheten. Du lider av høyt blodtrykk. Er det riktig? Hvis det er riktig, løft hånden.
Her er en annen ting. Du er ikke herfra. Du kommer fra New York City, et sted der oppe i det området. Du kom denne veien. Du har vært på møtet før. Du var på et møte, og det var... Jeg ser fru Brown, en eldre kvinne, stående. Det var på et møte i New York. Det var mitt møte og du ble helbredet. Og du hadde kreft, ikke sant? Og du er helbredet for høyt blodtrykk nå. Gå på din vei med glede. Jesus Kristus gjør deg hel.

text:
Do you believe Him to be the Son of God? Hallelujah! Amazing grace, how sweet the sound. Have faith in God. Don't doubt. Believe all things. If you can believe it, God can do it.
That little girl sitting there has kidney trouble, doesn't she? You're her grandmother aren't you? There's the children right behind you, that has kidney trouble too, is that right? You're the grandmother of the child. Put your hands over on them.
Almighty God, I condemn that devil. Come out of them in the name of Jesus Christ. They are babies. You leave them.
Don't fear. Have faith. Believe.
text_no:
Tror du at Han er Guds Sønn? Halleluja! For en vidunderlig nåde, så herlig en lyd. Ha tro på Gud. Ikke tvil. Tro alt. Hvis du kan tro det, kan Gud gjøre det.
Den lille jenta som sitter der, har nyreproblemer, ikke sant? Du er bestemoren hennes, er du ikke? Barna rett bak deg har også nyreproblemer, stemmer det? Du er bestemoren til barnet. Legg hendene dine på dem.
Allmektige Gud, jeg fordømmer denne djevelen. Kom ut av dem i Jesu Kristi navn. De er barn. Du skal forlate dem.
Ikke frykt. Ha tro. Tro.

text:
Lady sitting right there has a back trouble, don't you, lady? Sitting right here, right in there, have a back trouble. Jesus Christ makes you whole now, you can go home. You don't have no prayer card, or nothing else. You don't need any. Just go home, be well. Jesus Christ heals you.
Sitting right here, you have a kidney trouble, also. The lady, had a heart trouble with it, that lady with the feather in her hat. Isn't that right, lady? A kidney and heart trouble. If that's right, raise up to your feet. You accept Jesus as your healer? It turns light around you.
How am I knowing these things? There stands that pillar of fire. Looks like all of you could see it. It's right above the lady's head. There it is, standing there. Go home, lady. Believe in the Lord Jesus Christ and get well. Hallelujah!
You see what I mean? It's nothing vulgar. It's the power of the resurrected Jesus Christ. Look and live. Believe with all your heart.
text_no:
Damen som sitter rett der har ryggproblemer, har du ikke, dame? Rett her inne, har du ryggproblemer. Jesus Kristus gjør deg frisk nå, du kan gå hjem. Du har ingen bønnekort eller noe annet. Du trenger ikke noe slikt. Bare gå hjem og bli frisk. Jesus Kristus helbreder deg.
Sitter rett her, du har også nyreproblemer. Damen med en fjær i hatten, har også hjerteproblemer. Er det riktig, dame? Nyre- og hjerteproblemer. Hvis det stemmer, reis deg. Aksepterer du Jesus som din helbreder? Det lyser rundt deg.
Hvordan vet jeg disse tingene? Der står ildsøylen. Det ser ut som dere alle kunne se den. Den er rett over damens hode. Der står den. Gå hjem, dame. Tro på Herren Jesus Kristus og bli frisk. Halleluja!
Ser dere hva jeg mener? Det er ingenting vulgært. Det er kraften fra den oppstandne Jesus Kristus. Se og lev. Tro med hele ditt hjerte.

text:
What do you think about it, lady? Sitting there with a red dress on. There's a dark shadow between you and I. The Holy Spirit is speaking to me. It's positive. It's true. You're bothered with a nervous condition. Isn't that right? You with a… Yes, sir. You know what it is? It's a change of life; it's menopause, or at least that's what your doctor said. If that's right, raise up your hand like this and shake it. That's right.
You're healed. Jesus Christ makes you whole. Your faith touched Him, like the woman with the hem of the garment.
Oh, challenge your faith! Look and live. Praise be to God!
There He goes, moving right over to this corner, to the lady standing there. You got something wrong with your back, lady. It's in your spine. Stand up to your feet just a minute. I see it's in your back. It's in your spine. Isn't that right? You believe me to be God's prophet? You do? Doctor can't do nothing about that, can He? He tried, but he failed. There's nothing could be done.
text_no:
Hva synes du om det, frue? Du som sitter der med en rød kjole. Det er en mørk skygge mellom oss. Den Hellige Ånd taler til meg. Det er positivt. Det er sant. Du er plaget av en nervøs lidelse. Er ikke det riktig? Ja, du med… Ja, frue. Vet du hva det er? Det er en overgang i livet; det er overgangsalder, eller i det minste er det hva legen din sa. Hvis det er riktig, løft opp hånden slik og rist den. Det stemmer.
Du er helbredet. Jesus Kristus gjør deg hel. Din tro rørte ved Ham, som kvinnen med fliken av Kappen.
Å, utfordre din tro! Se og lev. Pris være Gud!
Der går Han, beveger Seg rett over til dette hjørnet, til damen som står der. Du har noe galt med ryggen, frue. Det er i ryggraden. Stå opp et øyeblikk. Jeg ser det er i ryggen. Det er i ryggraden. Er ikke det riktig? Tror du jeg er Guds profet? Du gjør? Legen kan ikke gjøre noe med det, kan Han? Han prøvde, men mislyktes. Det er ingenting som kan gjøres.

text:
Here---that you might know I'm God's prophet, you are … you got a mother, and she has something wrong with her eyes. It's the cataracts on her eyes. She's sitting in front of you. Isn't that right? It's your mother. Isn't that true? That's right.
Say, you answer… Your name is Mae, your first name. Your last name is Hall, isn't it? Yes, sir. Don't you live 74 Broad Street, here? All right. Lay your hand over on your mother. Satan, you're defeated in the name of Jesus Christ, come out of the woman.
You think I'm crazy. I'm not! The Holy Spirit is here, predominating.
text_no:
Her, for at du skal vite at jeg er Guds profet: Du har en mor, og hun har problemer med øynene. Det er grå stær på øynene hennes. Hun sitter foran deg. Er ikke det riktig? Det er din mor, er det ikke sant? Ja, det stemmer.
Ditt navn er Mae, ditt fornavn. Ditt etternavn er Hall, er det ikke? Ja, det er riktig. Bor du ikke på 74 Broad Street, her? Greit. Legg hånden din på din mor. Satan, du er beseiret i Jesu Kristi navn, kom ut av kvinnen.
Du tror jeg er gal, men det er jeg ikke! Den Hellige Ånd er her og dominerer.

text:
What do you think about it, lady? You had your hand laying on her then, on your friend there, sitting next to her. You have headaches all the time, don't you? Stand up on your feet. You had your hand on the woman; I seen that Angel jump from her to you. That's right. You have persistent headaches. Starts in the back of your neck, coming up. Just the time of life you're going through, also. Don't worry about it. It's going to leave you. I see you later on. You're not … you ain't got nothing on your head then. You're all right. You're going to be well. That's right. Do you believe it?
I see you … you're going into a house by the number of 110, that's your number, 110 Moore Street, or something like that. Isn't that right? You answer by the name of Alice, don't you? Raise up your hand and answer to Jesus Christ, as His servant; healed. Hallelujah!
Do you believe Him? Stand to your feet, every one of you, right now. It's moving all over you. Everywhere, accept your healing right now.
Lord God, in the name of Jesus, the Son of God, I cast out every unclean spirit.
text_no:
Hva mener du om dette, frue? Du holdt hånden din på henne, på din venn som sitter ved siden av deg. Du har hodepine hele tiden, ikke sant? Reis deg. Jeg så Engelen hoppe fra henne til deg. Det stemmer. Du har vedvarende hodepine som starter i nakken og går oppover. Det er også på grunn av den livsfasen du går gjennom. Ikke bekymre deg. Det kommer til å forlate deg. Jeg ser deg senere, og du har ingenting på hodet da. Du er frisk. Du kommer til å bli bra. Tror du på det?
Jeg ser deg ... du går inn i et hus med nummer 110, det er ditt nummer, 110 Moore Street eller noe lignende. Stemmer ikke det? Du heter Alice, gjør du ikke? Løft hånden og svar til Jesus Kristus, som Hans tjener; du er helbredet. Halleluja!
Tror du på Ham? Reis dere alle sammen, akkurat nå. Det rører seg overalt blant dere. Ta imot helbredelsen nå.
Herre Gud, i Jesu, Guds Sønns, navn, kaster jeg ut hver uren ånd.
